summary: Web-research model architectures, hardware specs, framework memory behavior, and VRAM formula — each result adversarially verified
logs: Fanning out 19 research agents, each followed by an adversarial verifier
Major corrections needed on: model:Gemma (Google) — user asked for "Gemma 4", model:DeepSeek

research.family: Qwen3.5 / Qwen3.6 (current generation of the Qwen3 family, Alibaba)
research.family_notes: NAMING REALITY CHECK: 'Qwen3' (Apr 2025; dense 0.6B-32B + MoE 30B-A3B/235B-A22B, refreshed as -2507) exists but was superseded. Current generation as of June 2026: Qwen3.5 (released 2026-02-16; dense 0.8B/2B/4B/9B/27B + MoE 35B-A3B/122B-A10B/397B-A17B) and Qwen3.6 refreshes (Apr 2026; only 27B and 35B-A3B refreshed, Apache 2.0, identical architecture/param counts to their 3.5 counterparts). Qwen3.5-Omni and Qwen3.6-Plus are proprietary/API-only (excluded). ATTENTION ARCHITECTURE (load-bearing for KV math): every 3.5/3.6 variant, dense AND MoE, is a hybrid linear+full stack with full_attention_interval=4 — layer_types repeats [linear_attention x3, full_attention x1]. ONLY num_hidden_layers/4 layers have a growing KV cache (gated full attention, GQA, head_dim=256, num_key_value_heads=2 or 4). KV bytes/token = 2 * (num_layers/4) * num_kv_heads * 256 * dtype_bytes (e.g. 397B-A17B at bf16: 2*15*2*256*2 = 30.7 KB/token). The other 3/4 layers are Gated DeltaNet linear attention with CONSTANT per-sequence recurrent state (linear_num_key_heads=16, key_head_dim=128, value heads 16-64 x 128, conv kernel 4; mamba_ssm_dtype=float32) — does not scale with context. No sliding-window attention anywhere (swa=null); no MLA. partial_rotary_factor=0.25 with interleaved M-RoPE. MULTIMODAL CAVEAT: all repos are Qwen3_5(Moe)ForConditionalGeneration with a built-in vision encoder (~0.1-0.4B) plus 1 MTP draft layer; these ARE the mainline text/instruct models (no text-only checkpoints this generation) and params_total_b below is the full safetensors count actually loaded into VRAM, which exceeds the marketing number (e.g. '397B' repo = 403.4B weights). params_active_b uses the marketing active-text count (A3B/A10B/A17B). Context: 262,144 native, YaRN-extendable to 1,010,000 per official model cards. Native dtype bfloat16 (tiny fp32 norm tensors); official FP8 and GPTQ-Int4 repos exist for 27B/35B/122B/397B sizes. PRIOR-GEN QUICK REFERENCE (if the calculator also lists legacy Qwen3, verified from configs): standard GQA on every layer, head_dim=128, no SWA; dense (0.6B-32B) num_kv_heads=8, 28-64 layers, max_position_embeddings 40960 (base) or 262144 (-2507); MoE Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 (48L, kv=4) and Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 (94L, kv=4) at 262144 ctx; Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B was the architectural bridge (48L, full attn every 4th, kv=2, head_dim=256).

missing: Base checkpoints exist for Qwen3.5-0.8B/2B/4B/9B and Qwen3.5-35B-A3B (Qwen/Qwen3.5-*-Base); identical architecture/VRAM footprint to instruct, but a calculator listing repos may want them. No Base repos exist for 27B/122B/397B.

Qwen3.6 official quantized repos are FP8 ONLY (Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8, Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8, both verified to exist); there is NO Qwen3.6 GPTQ-Int4 repo as of 2026-06-12, unlike Qwen3.5 where GPTQ-Int4 exists for all four large sizes (created 2026-03-03). The researcher's FP8/GPTQ sentence is correct for Qwen3.5 but readers could over-generalize it to 3.6.

Qwen3-Coder-Next (Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next, released 2026-01-30, plus -Base/-FP8/-GGUF repos) - a current coder model on the Qwen3-Next 80B-A3B hybrid architecture, released between Qwen3 and Qwen3.5; omitted from both the current-gen table and the prior-gen quick reference. Legacy Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B / Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B also unmentioned.

Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B (open-weights, Sep 2025) omitted from the prior-gen reference; note the researcher correctly states Qwen3.5-Omni is API-only (confirmed: Alibaba kept Qwen3.5-Omni closed-source, Plus/Flash/Light API variants only).
Qwen3.5-Plus is the hosted/API counterpart of Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (per the official 397B model card) - worth noting alongside the Omni/3.6-Plus exclusions so users do not look for its weights.

VRAM-relevant serving nuance from official model cards: vLLM/SGLang support a --language-model-only flag that skips loading the vision tower (~0.09B params on 0.8B/2B with 12-layer/768-hidden ViT, ~0.4B on 27B+/MoE with 27-layer/1152-hidden ViT), and the 1 MTP draft layer included in the safetensors total is only loaded when speculative decoding is enabled - so the true minimum weight footprint is slightly below params_total_b.

YaRN long-context detail useful for the calculator: official cards specify factor 4.0 with original_max_position_embeddings=262144 to reach 1,010,000 - and only the num_layers/4 full-attention layers grow KV with that context; the linear-attention recurrent state stays constant.
verification.summary: Adversarial re-verification of all 10 variant rows plus family notes, fetched independently from primary sources (raw HuggingFace config.json files, HF model API safetensors counts, official model card READMEs). Result: ZERO numeric errors found - this is an unusually accurate dataset. Verified exactly: (1) every config field for all 10 rows - num_hidden_layers (24/24/32/32/64/64/40/40/48/60), num_attention_heads (8/8/16/16/24/24/16/16/32/32), num_key_value_heads (2/2/4/4/4/4/2/2/2/2), head_dim=256, hidden_size (1024/2048/2560/4096/5120/5120/2048/2048/3072/4096), full_attention_interval=4 with layer_types counts confirming KV cache on exactly num_layers/4 layers (6/24, 8/32, 16/64, 10/40, 12/48, 15/60), max_position_embeddings=262144, dtype bfloat16, sliding_window=null, no MLA; (2) MoE params - 35B/122B: 256 experts, 8 per token, shared expert present; 397B: 512 experts, 10 per token, shared expert present; (3) safetensors totals via HF API: 0.8734B, 2.2741B, 4.6599B, 9.6531B, 27.7814B, 35.9518B, 125.0865B, 403.3979B - all match the rounded params_total_b values, and Qwen3.6-27B/35B-A3B totals are byte-identical to their 3.5 counterparts, confirming the identical-architecture refresh claim; (4) linear-attention block - linear_num_key_heads=16, key/value head_dim=128, value heads 16/32/48/64 across sizes, conv kernel 4, mamba_ssm_dtype=float32, partial_rotary_factor=0.25, mrope_interleaved=true; (5) KV bytes/token formula and the 30.7 KB/token example for 397B (2*15*2*256*2=30720 B) are arithmetically and architecturally correct; (6) context extension - '262,144 natively and extensible up to 1,010,000 tokens' appears verbatim in official 397B and Qwen3.6-27B model cards (YaRN factor 4.0); (7) timeline/licensing - Qwen3.5-397B created 2026-02-16, medium series 2026-02-24/25, small dense 2026-02-26..28, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B 2026-04-15 and 3.6-27B 2026-04-21, all license:apache-2.0 per HF API; only 27B and 35B-A3B were refreshed in 3.6 (org enumeration confirms no other Qwen3.6 repos); (8) FP8+GPTQ-Int4 official repos exist for Qwen3.5 27B/35B/122B/397B; (9) architectures are Qwen3_5(Moe)ForConditionalGeneration with vision tower and mtp_num_hidden_layers=1, confirming no text-only checkpoints; (10) prior-gen reference spot-checks all pass - Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 (48L, kv=4, hd=128, 262144), Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 (94L, kv=4), Qwen3-32B (64L, kv=8, hd=128, 40960), Qwen3-0.6B (28L), Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B (48L, kv=2, hd=256, full_attention_interval=4); (11) Qwen3.5-Omni confirmed closed-source/API-only via news coverage and absence from the HF org. Key sources: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B/raw/main/config.json, https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B/raw/main/config.json, https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B/raw/main/config.json, https://huggingface.co/api/models/Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B?expand[]=safetensors, https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B/raw/main/README.md, https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3.6. The 'missing' list contains only additive items (Base checkpoints, Qwen3-Coder-Next, Qwen3.6-FP8-only nuance, vision-tower/MTP load-skipping flags); none invalidate any published number. Safe to ingest as-is.

research.family_notes: NAMING REALITY CHECK: 'Gemma 4' DOES exist as of June 2026. Released April 2, 2026 (Apache 2.0), with the 12B 'unified' variant added June 3, 2026. It supersedes Gemma 3 (March 2025) and Gemma 3n (June 2025). Five sizes: E2B, E4B, 12B, 26B-A4B (MoE), 31B (dense flagship). ATTENTION ARCHITECTURE (critical for KV math): all variants interleave local sliding-window attention with full global attention, final layer always global. E2B uses a 4:1 local:global pattern (full attention at layer indices 4,9,...,34 -> 7 of 35 layers global); E4B/12B/26B/31B use 5:1 (full attention every 6th layer: E4B 7/42, 12B 8/48, 26B 5/30, 31B 10/60 global layers). Sliding window = 512 tokens (E2B/E4B) or 1024 tokens (12B/26B/31B). LOAD-BEARING KV-CACHE QUIRKS unique to Gemma 4: (1) Global (full-attention) layers use a DIFFERENT head dim and KV head count than sliding layers: global_head_dim=512 (vs head_dim=256 on sliding layers) and num_global_key_value_heads (12B: 1, 26B: 2, 31B: 4; E2B/E4B: null -> falls back to num_key_value_heads). So per-token KV bytes differ per layer type: sliding layer = num_key_value_heads*256, global layer = num_global_key_value_heads*512. (2) attention_k_eq_v=true on 12B/26B/31B: key projection output is reused as the value projection (per transformers configuration_gemma4.py docs), so the cache stores only K, not K+V — HALVES KV cache vs standard GQA. attention_k_eq_v=false on E2B/E4B. (3) E2B/E4B instead have num_kv_shared_layers=20/18: the last N consecutive decoder layers share KV projections (Gemma 3n-style), so only 35-20=15 (E2B) / 42-18=24 (E4B) layers contribute distinct KV cache. (4) E2B/E4B use Per-Layer Embeddings (PLE): raw safetensors are 5.12B/8.0B params but effective accelerator-resident footprint is ~2.3B/4.5B. (5) Global layers use Proportional RoPE (p-RoPE, partial_rotary_factor 0.25, theta 1e6); sliding layers theta 1e4. Context is native 262144 (256K) for 12B/26B/31B and 131072 (128K) for E2B/E4B per config max_position_embeddings (no RoPE-extension discrepancy in sources). All weights ship bfloat16; official QAT q4_0-GGUF and w4a16 repos exist per size. Safetensors totals include the ~1-1.5B vision tower (e.g. 31B-it = 32.68B safetensors vs 30.7B text-only per model card); param counts below are full-checkpoint safetensors totals. All variants are multimodal-in/text-out (text+image, audio on E2B/E4B/12B) — included here because they ARE the text-generation models of this family; there are no text-only releases.

variants.name: Gemma 4 E2B Instruct
variants.attention_type: gqa+swa (interleaved sliding window, 4:1 local:global; global layers use global_head_dim=512; last 20 layers share KV projections via num_kv_shared_layers)

swa.pattern_note: 4 sliding + 1 full repeating; full attention at indices 4,9,14,19,24,29,34 (7 of 35); final layer global. Global layers: head_dim 512, KV heads = num_key_value_heads (1) since num_global_key_value_heads is null. num_kv_shared_layers=20 -> only 15 layers hold distinct KV cache. attention_k_eq_v=false (separate K and V). Per-Layer Embeddings: 5.12B raw, ~2.3B effective.

variants.name: Gemma 4 E4B Instruct
variants.attention_type: gqa+swa (interleaved sliding window, 5:1 local:global; global layers use global_head_dim=512; last 18 layers share KV projections)

swa.pattern_note: 5 sliding + 1 full repeating; full attention at indices 5,11,...,41 (7 of 42); final layer global. Global layers: head_dim 512, KV heads = num_key_value_heads (2) since num_global_key_value_heads is null. num_kv_shared_layers=18 -> only 24 layers hold distinct KV cache. attention_k_eq_v=false. Per-Layer Embeddings: 8.0B raw, ~4.5B effective.

variants.name: Gemma 4 12B Instruct (unified encoder-free multimodal)
variants.attention_type: gqa+swa (interleaved sliding window, 5:1 local:global; K=V unified projection halves KV cache; global layers: 1 KV head at head_dim 512)

swa.pattern_note: 5 sliding + 1 full repeating; full attention at indices 5,11,...,47 (8 of 48); final layer global. Sliding layers: 8 KV heads x 256 dim; global layers: num_global_key_value_heads=1 x global_head_dim=512. attention_k_eq_v=true -> cache stores K only (V=K), halving KV bytes. num_kv_shared_layers=0. Released June 3, 2026; architecture Gemma4UnifiedForConditionalGeneration (encoder-free vision/audio via linear projections).

variants.name: Gemma 4 26B A4B Instruct (MoE)
variants.attention_type: gqa+swa (interleaved sliding window, 5:1 local:global; K=V unified projection; global layers: 2 KV heads at head_dim 512)

swa.pattern_note: 5 sliding + 1 full repeating; full attention at indices 5,11,17,23,29 (5 of 30); final layer global. Sliding layers: 8 KV heads x 256 dim; global layers: num_global_key_value_heads=2 x global_head_dim=512. attention_k_eq_v=true -> K-only cache. MoE: 128 experts, top-8 routing + 1 shared expert; moe_intermediate_size=704, shared/dense intermediate_size=2112. 26.54B safetensors total (model card text-only: 25.2B), ~3.8-4B active/token.

variants.name: Gemma 4 31B Instruct (dense flagship)
variants.attention_type: gqa+swa (interleaved sliding window, 5:1 local:global; K=V unified projection; global layers: 4 KV heads at head_dim 512)

swa.pattern_note: 5 sliding + 1 full repeating; full attention at indices 5,11,...,59 (10 of 60); final layer global. Sliding layers: 16 KV heads x 256 dim; global layers: num_global_key_value_heads=4 x global_head_dim=512. attention_k_eq_v=true -> K-only cache. 32.68B safetensors total incl. vision tower (model card text-only: 30.7B). #3 open model on LMArena text per Google launch blog.

corrections.claim: Gemma 4 31B Instruct params_total_b
corrections.should_be: 31.27 (safetensors BF16 parameter count = 31,273,088,876; on-disk safetensors = 62,546,338,248 bytes = 2 bytes/param, confirming ~31.27B stored weights / ~62.5 GB BF16). The 32.68B figure is the HF API metadata 'total' field (32,682,372,656), which exceeds the per-dtype BF16 sum by ~1.409B — almost exactly vocab_size*hidden_size (262144*5376), i.e. a tied-embedding double count in the metadata, not real weights. Using 32.68B overestimates weight VRAM by ~2.8 GB at BF16.

corrections.claim: Gemma 4 26B A4B Instruct params_total_b
corrections.should_be: 25.81 (safetensors BF16 parameter count = 25,805,936,206; on-disk safetensors = 51,612,009,916 bytes ≈ 2 bytes/param). 26.54B is again the inflated HF API 'total' (26,544,131,376), exceeding the BF16 sum by ~0.738B ≈ vocab_size*hidden_size (262144*2816) tied-embedding double count. Real BF16 weight footprint ≈ 51.6 GB.

corrections.claim: family_notes quirk (2): attention_k_eq_v=true means 'the cache stores only K, not K+V — HALVES KV cache vs standard GQA' (model-wide on 12B/26B/31B)
corrections.was: K=V / K-only cache applied to ALL layers of 12B/26B/31B, halving total KV cache
corrections.should_be: K=V applies ONLY to full/global-attention layers. transformers modeling_gemma4.py and modeling_gemma4_unified.py both define: `self.use_alternative_attention = config.attention_k_eq_v and not self.is_sliding`; v_proj is None only on those layers. Sliding-window layers on 12B/26B/31B keep a separate v_proj and store standard K+V. Additionally, the HF reference implementation passes the aliased key tensor into the standard DynamicCache.update (no custom cache class), so even on global layers transformers materializes both K and V copies — the 'halving' on global layers is only realizable by engines that exploit V≡K.

corrections.claim: family_notes per-token KV bytes formula: 'sliding layer = num_key_value_heads*256, global layer = num_global_key_value_heads*512'
corrections.was: sliding layer = num_key_value_heads*256 (implicitly K-only on k_eq_v models)
corrections.should_be: Per-token cached elements: sliding layer = num_key_value_heads * 256 * 2 (K AND V — always, on every variant, since k_eq_v never applies to sliding layers); global layer on 12B/26B/31B = num_global_key_value_heads * 512 * 1 (K only, V aliased — engine-dependent, 2x in HF transformers); global layer on E2B/E4B = num_key_value_heads * 512 * 2 (k_eq_v=false). As written, the formula undercounts sliding-layer KV (the dominant layer type, e.g. 40/48 layers on 12B) by 2x on 12B/26B/31B.

corrections.claim: family_notes: 'official QAT q4_0-GGUF and w4a16 repos exist per size'
corrections.was: w4a16 QAT repos exist for every size
corrections.should_be: qat-q4_0-gguf exists for all five sizes, but -qat-w4a16-ct exists only for E2B, E4B, 12B, and 31B — there is NO google/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-qat-w4a16-ct in the google org listing.

corrections.claim: family_notes: '31B-it = 32.68B safetensors vs 30.7B text-only' and 'safetensors totals include the ~1-1.5B vision tower'
corrections.was: vision tower accounts for the gap between 32.68B safetensors and 30.7B text-only
corrections.should_be: Actual safetensors param count is 31.27B vs 30.7B text-only (model card), so the multimodal (vision) overhead is ~0.57B for 31B and ~0.61B for 26B (25.81B vs 25.2B text-only) — not ~1-1.5B. The larger gaps the researcher saw are the tied-embedding metadata double count, not vision weights.

corrections.claim: family_notes: 'Released April 2, 2026'
corrections.was: April 2, 2026
corrections.should_be: Official Gemma releases changelog (ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases) lists 'March 31, 2026: Release of Gemma 4 in E2B, E4B, 31B and 26B A4B sizes'. April 2 matches the public announcement push (Google AI X post / Cloud blog), so the discrepancy is 2 days and immaterial to VRAM math; the June 3, 2026 date for the 12B Unified is confirmed by the same page.
corrections.confidence: medium (changelog is primary; multiple secondary sources say April 2 for the announcement)

missing: MTP (multi-token prediction) speculative-decoding drafter models, released April 16, 2026 per the official releases page ('Release of Gemma 4 - MTP for E2B, E4B, 31B, and 26B A4B'): shipped as '-assistant' repos, e.g. google/gemma-4-31B-it-assistant (architecture Gemma4AssistantForCausalLM, 469,518,596 BF16 params ≈ 0.94 GB). Exists for every size incl. 12B, plus -qat-q4_0-unquantized-assistant variants. Relevant to a VRAM calculator: running speculative decoding adds drafter weights + drafter cache. Sources: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases, https://huggingface.co/api/models/google/gemma-4-31B-it-assistant, https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/mtp/overview

Base (pretrained, non-instruct) checkpoints exist for all five sizes: google/gemma-4-E2B, gemma-4-E4B, gemma-4-12B, gemma-4-26B-A4B, gemma-4-31B (same shapes/sizes as -it). Source: https://huggingface.co/api/models?author=google&search=gemma-4

QAT mobile variants for the edge sizes: google/gemma-4-E2B-it-qat-mobile-transformers, -qat-mobile-ct, and E4B equivalents — distinct from q4_0/w4a16 and relevant for on-device memory estimates. Source: https://huggingface.co/api/models?author=google&search=gemma-4

-qat-q4_0-unquantized repos (BF16 weights of the QAT run, intended for user-side quantization) for all five sizes — useful if the calculator lists official quantization paths. Source: https://huggingface.co/api/models?author=google&search=gemma-4
Audio caveat worth encoding: audio input is capped at 30 seconds and only on E2B/E4B/12B (researcher stated the sizes but not the 30s cap). Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/core/model_card_4
verification.summary: Gemma 4 is real (initial release March 31/April 2, 2026; 12B Unified added June 3, 2026) and the researcher's architecture data is largely accurate — I independently re-fetched all five HF config.json files and the transformers gemma4/gemma4_unified modeling source. VERIFIED CLEAN: all num_layers (35/42/48/30/60), hidden_size, num_attn_heads, num_kv_heads (1/2/8/8/16), head_dim 256 + global_head_dim 512, num_global_key_value_heads (null/null/1/2/4), attention_k_eq_v flags (false/false/true/true/true), num_kv_shared_layers (20/18/0/0/0) with last-N-layers-never-update-cache semantics confirmed in code, sliding_window 512/512/1024/1024/1024, exact layer_types patterns and global-layer indices (7/35 at 4,9..34; 7/42, 8/48, 5/30, 10/60 at every 6th), max contexts 131072/262144, p-RoPE (partial_rotary_factor 0.25, theta 1e6 global / 1e4 sliding), BF16 native, MoE 128 experts top-8 + 1 shared, moe_intermediate_size 704, intermediate 2112, 3.8B active, PLE effective 2.3B/4.5B, audio on E2B/E4B/12B, E2B/E4B/12B param totals (5.12B/8.0B/11.96B), Apache 2.0, q4_0 GGUF QAT per size, all hf_ids. TWO LOAD-BEARING ERRORS: (1) 26B and 31B params_total_b (26.54/32.68) come from an inflated HF API 'total' metadata field that double-counts the tied embedding; actual stored weights are 25.81B and 31.27B (confirmed by on-disk safetensors byte sums 51.61 GB / 62.55 GB at 2 bytes/param). (2) The attention_k_eq_v 'halves KV cache' claim is over-broad: code shows `use_alternative_attention = attention_k_eq_v and not is_sliding`, so V=K applies ONLY to global layers; sliding layers (the vast majority) store full K+V on all variants, and even on global layers the HF reference implementation materializes both copies (no dedup cache class) — the researcher's per-token formula undercounts sliding-layer KV by 2x on 12B/26B/31B. Lesser fixes: no w4a16 QAT repo for 26B-A4B; vision-tower overhead is ~0.6B not 1-1.5B; launch changelog says March 31 not April 2. Missed: MTP '-assistant' drafter repos (~0.47B params each, all sizes), base PT checkpoints, QAT-mobile E2B/E4B variants. The '#3 on LMArena' marketing claim was not verified (immaterial to VRAM). Key sources: huggingface.co/google/gemma-4-*-it config.json + /api/models trees, ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/core/model_card_4, ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases, github.com/huggingface/transformers src/transformers/models/gemma4{,_unified}/modeling_*.py.

results.key: model:GLM (Zhipu AI / Z.ai) — user asked for "GLM-5"

research.family_notes: NAMING REALITY CHECK: GLM-5 DOES exist as of June 2026. Z.ai (Zhipu AI, HF org `zai-org`) launched GLM-5 on Feb 11, 2026 with open MIT-licensed weights on HuggingFace, and the current flagship is GLM-5.1 (released Apr 7, 2026). Between GLM-4.6 (Oct 2025) and GLM-5 there was also GLM-4.7 (Dec 22, 2025) and GLM-4.7-Flash (Jan 2026). TWO ATTENTION ARCHITECTURES coexist in the current lineup — do NOT use one KV formula for the whole family: (1) `glm4_moe` (GLM-4.5, GLM-4.5-Air, GLM-4.6, GLM-4.7) is standard GQA: 96 attn heads / 8 KV heads / head_dim 128, partial_rotary_factor 0.5 (rotary applied to half of head_dim — does NOT change KV cache size) -> KV cache = 2 * 8 * 128 = 2048 elements/token/layer. (2) `glm_moe_dsa` (GLM-5, GLM-5.1) and `glm4_moe_lite` (GLM-4.7-Flash) are DeepSeek-style MLA: compressed KV cache = kv_lora_rank(512) + qk_rope_head_dim(64) = 576 elements/token/layer. CAUTION: the MLA configs still list `num_key_value_heads` equal to `num_attention_heads` (64 for GLM-5, 20 for 4.7-Flash) — that is a formality; using it for GQA-style KV math would overestimate cache by ~50x. GLM-5/5.1 additionally integrate DSA (DeepSeek Sparse Attention): a lightning indexer (index_n_heads 32, index_head_dim 128, index_topk 2048) adds a small per-token indexer key cache (~128 elements/layer, stored fp8 in reference impls) on top of the 576-element MLA latent; attention compute is sparse (top-2048 tokens) but the full MLA cache is still resident. No sliding-window attention anywhere in the family. MTP: GLM-5/5.1 and the GLM-4.x flagships ship one multi-token-prediction layer (num_nextn_predict_layers=1), which is why HF safetensors badges (754B for GLM-5/5.1; 357-358B for 4.6/4.7) exceed official marketing figures (744B-A40B per zai-org/GLM-5 GitHub README; 355B-A32B for the 4.x flagships) — a VRAM calculator should use the safetensors total if MTP weights are loaded (vLLM/SGLang speculative decoding), else the official figure. GLM-4.5/4.6/4.7 share a byte-identical architecture (92 layers, 160 experts, hidden 5120); 4.6/4.7 only extend context 131072 -> 202752 and improve post-training. NO Air or Flash variant exists for the GLM-5 generation as of 2026-06-12 (open community request: huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-5/discussions/3); the smallest current-gen local options are GLM-4.7-Flash (31B-A3B, MLA) and GLM-4.5-Air (106B-A12B, GQA). Official FP8 repos (GLM-5-FP8, GLM-5.1-FP8) are e4m3 block-quantized [128,128], halving weight VRAM vs BF16.

corrections.claim: GLM-4.5 variant params_total_b
corrections.should_be: 358.3 (HF safetensors total = 358,337,791,296 BF16 params, identical to GLM-4.7's, because GLM-4.5 also ships the MTP layer with duplicated embed/lm_head). 355 is the marketing figure (744B-blog: 'GLM-5 scales from 355B parameters (32B active)'). The table is internally inconsistent: it uses safetensors-basis numbers for 4.6 (357) and 4.7 (358) but marketing-basis for 4.5 (355). Pick one basis; on the safetensors basis GLM-4.5 = 358.
corrections.confidence: high — fetched directly from HF API

corrections.claim: GLM-4.5-Air variant params_total_b
corrections.should_be: 110.5 on the safetensors basis (HF safetensors total = 110,468,824,832, incl. MTP layer); 106 is the official marketing figure (106B-A12B). Same basis inconsistency as GLM-4.5 — if the calculator follows the family_notes' own rule (safetensors total when MTP weights are loaded), Air's loaded-weight figure is ~110.5B, a 4.5B/~9GB-BF16 discrepancy.
corrections.confidence: high — fetched directly from HF API

corrections.claim: family_notes: 'GLM-4.5/4.6/4.7 share a byte-identical architecture'
corrections.was: implies identical parameter counts across 4.5/4.6/4.7
corrections.should_be: configs are identical (except max_position_embeddings 131072 vs 202752), but safetensors param counts are NOT: 4.5 and 4.7 = 358.34B while 4.6 = 356.79B. The difference is exactly 2 x vocab x hidden = 151552 x 5120 x 2 = 1,551,892,480 — GLM-4.6's checkpoint does not store the duplicated MTP-layer embedding + lm_head that 4.5/4.7 store. Worth documenting so the 357-vs-358 badge difference isn't mistaken for an architecture change.
corrections.source: https://huggingface.co/api/models/zai-org/GLM-4.6 (356785898816) vs zai-org/GLM-4.5 and zai-org/GLM-4.7 (both 358337791296); configs at https://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-4.6/raw/main/config.json

missing: Official FP8 repos for the entire 4.x line, all exist and are directly relevant to a VRAM calculator: zai-org/GLM-4.7-FP8 (created 2025-12-22), zai-org/GLM-4.6-FP8 (2025-09-29), zai-org/GLM-4.5-FP8 and zai-org/GLM-4.5-Air-FP8 (2025-07-20). The researcher listed FP8 variants only for GLM-5/5.1. Note: NO official GLM-4.7-Flash-FP8 exists.
Base (non-instruct) checkpoints: zai-org/GLM-4.5-Base and zai-org/GLM-4.5-Air-Base (same VRAM profile as their chat counterparts).

Vision-language siblings if multimodal is in scope: zai-org/GLM-4.5V + GLM-4.5V-FP8 (106B-A12B, built on GLM-4.5-Air), zai-org/GLM-4.6V + GLM-4.6V-FP8 (built on GLM-4.6, created 2025-12-07), and zai-org/GLM-4.6V-Flash (small VLM).

GLM-5/5.1 MoE routing facts useful for offload-aware calculators (verified from config): n_routed_experts=256 + 1 shared, num_experts_per_tok=8, first_k_dense_replace=3 (first 3 layers dense), moe_intermediate_size=2048. GLM-4.7-Flash: 64 routed experts + 1 shared, 4 per token, first_k_dense_replace=1.
GLM-4.7-Flash uses attention_bias=false and partial_rotary_factor=1.0 (unlike glm4_moe's attention_bias=true / 0.5) — irrelevant to KV size but relevant if weight-counting per-layer.
verification.summary: Re-verified all 9 variants against freshly-fetched primary sources (HF raw config.json for every repo, HF API safetensors totals, official READMEs, zai-org full model listing). The research is highly accurate: GLM-5 and GLM-5.1 exist (repos created 2026-02-11 and 2026-04-03, public weights release ~Apr 7-8 per Z.ai release notes), both glm_moe_dsa with exactly the claimed numbers — 78 layers, hidden 6144, 64 heads, head_dim 64, kv_lora_rank 512, qk_rope_head_dim 64 (MLA cache 576 elem/tok/layer correct), qk_head_dim 256 (192 nope + 64 rope), v_head_dim 256, q_lora_rank 2048, DSA indexer index_n_heads 32 / index_head_dim 128 / index_topk 2048, num_key_value_heads=64 formality confirmed, max_position 202752, num_nextn_predict_layers=1, safetensors 753.86B (~754B) vs official 744B-A40B (confirmed verbatim in GLM-5 README: '744B parameters (40B active)'). FP8 repos confirmed e4m3 weight_block_size [128,128] (751.75B F8_E4M3 + 2.11B BF16 tensors). GLM-4.7/4.6/4.5 GQA configs verified exactly (92 layers, 96/8 heads, head_dim 128, partial_rotary_factor 0.5, 2048 elem/tok/layer KV; 202752 ctx for 4.6/4.7, 131072 for 4.5). GLM-4.7-Flash verified (glm4_moe_lite MLA, 47 layers, hidden 2048, 20 heads, kv_lora 512/rope 64, q_lora_rank 768, official '30B-A3B' confirmed in README, safetensors 31.22B). GLM-4.5-Air config verified (46 layers, hidden 4096, 96/8/128). No sliding-window fields in any config. No GLM-5-gen Air/Flash exists (full zai-org listing checked). Only issues found: params_total_b basis inconsistency — GLM-4.5 listed as 355 (marketing) while 4.6/4.7 use safetensors values, though GLM-4.5's safetensors total (358.34B) equals GLM-4.7's; GLM-4.5-Air listed as 106 vs 110.47B safetensors; and the '4.5/4.6/4.7 byte-identical' note glosses over GLM-4.6's checkpoint being 1.55B params smaller (omits duplicated MTP embed/head). Missed items: official FP8 repos for all 4.x models, Base checkpoints, and V-series multimodal siblings.

research.family: DeepSeek (V3 / R1 / V3.1 / V3.2 / V4 lineage)
research.family_notes: NAMING REALITY (verified against HF deepseek-ai org listing, June 2026): Current flagship generation is DeepSeek-V4 (V4-Pro 1.6T/49B-active, V4-Flash 284B/13B-active, released ~2026-04-24, 1M context) — this postdates the task's 'V3.2 or newer' assumption and is included. 'DeepSeek-R2' does NOT exist as of 2026-06-12 (not in HF org, not in API model list). The V3-architecture 671B lineage: V3 (2024-12), V3-0324, R1 (2025-01), R1-0528, V3.1 (2025-08), V3.1-Terminus (2025-09), V3.2-Exp (2025-09, first DSA), V3.2 + V3.2-Speciale (2025-12-01, stable DSA generation). Omitted as config-identical duplicates: DeepSeek-V3==V3-0324, R1==R1-0528, V3.1==V3.1-Terminus, V3.2-Exp==V3.2==V3.2-Speciale (Speciale is the high-compute reasoning tune of V3.2, same config.json; Exp differs from V3.2 only in transformers metadata), R1-Distill-Qwen-7B (7.6B, hidden 3584/28L/28H/4KV/131072 ctx) and R1-Distill-Llama-8B (8.0B, 32L/4096/32H/8KV) dropped to fit cap — the Qwen3-8B distill is the 8B people now run. ATTENTION/KV MATH — three regimes in this family: (1) MLA (V3, V3-0324, R1, R1-0528, V3.1, V3.1-Terminus): KV cache per token per layer = kv_lora_rank + qk_rope_head_dim = 512 + 64 = 576 elements TOTAL (shared across all 128 query heads — do NOT multiply by num_kv_heads; config's num_key_value_heads=128 is vestigial and must be ignored). All 671B models: 61 layers, so 576*61 = 35,136 elements/token (~70.3 KB BF16, ~35.1 KB FP8 cache). Other MLA dims: q_lora_rank=1536, qk_nope_head_dim=128, v_head_dim=128 (per-head q/k dim is 192=128nope+64rope; head_dim field set to null in rows below because hidden/heads=56 is meaningless for MLA). (2) MLA+DSA (V3.2 family): identical MLA latent cache (576/token/layer) PLUS a lightning-indexer key cache (index_n_heads=64, index_head_dim=128, index_topk=2048 — adds ~128 FP8 elements/token/layer in serving engines); DSA cuts attention FLOPs (each token attends to top-2048), KV cache RESIDENCY unchanged vs MLA. (3) V4 hybrid CSA+HCA (model_type deepseek_v4, NOT MLA — no kv_lora_rank): MQA-style single shared KV head, num_key_value_heads=1, head_dim=512 plus qk_rope_head_dim=64 → uncompressed cost ~576 elem/token/layer like MLA, but per-layer 'compress_ratios' (Pro: [128,128,4,128,4,...,128,4,128,0]; Flash: [0,0,4,128,...,4,0]; length = layers+1, last entry is the MTP head) compress the KV cache 4x on CSA layers and 128x on HCA layers (0 = uncompressed); config sliding_window=128 is the local uncompressed window inside this scheme, NOT Gemma-style interleaved SWA. DeepSeek states V4-Pro needs only ~10% of V3.2's KV cache at 1M ctx — a calculator must special-case this, not reuse MLA or GQA formulas. WEIGHTS/DTYPE: all 671B models ship native FP8 (block e4m3; config torch_dtype=bfloat16 is the compute dtype) ≈ 671 GB on disk (685B params counting the bundled MTP module). V4 ships FP4 MoE experts + FP8 elsewhere; V4-Flash safetensors total = 159.6 GB verified; V4-Pro ~0.9 TB (1.6T params, not directly verified — index file not readable). V4 extras for completeness: hyper-connections (hc_mult=4), num_hash_layers=3, scoring_func=sqrtsoftplus, n_routed_experts 384(Pro)/256(Flash), top-6 routing. CONTEXT: all 671B models max_position_embeddings=163840 via YaRN (factor 40 from native 4096; official API serves 128K). V4: 1,048,576 via YaRN factor 16 from 65,536 native. Distills are plain GQA (Qwen2.5/Qwen3/Llama3 backbones) — standard 2*num_kv_heads*head_dim per layer; the Qwen2-based distills have sliding_window keys in config but use_sliding_window=false, so treat as pure GQA.

variants.name: DeepSeek-V4-Flash
variants.attention_type: linear/other — DeepSeek-V4 hybrid CSA+HCA (model_type deepseek_v4, NOT MLA): single shared KV head (num_key_value_heads=1, head_dim=512) + qk_rope_head_dim=64, with per-layer KV compression and DSA-style indexer (index_n_heads=64, index_head_dim=128, index_topk=512)

swa.pattern_note: NOT Gemma-style interleaved SWA. config sliding_window=128 is the local uncompressed window inside compressed attention. compress_ratios (len 44 = 43 layers + 1 MTP head): [0,0,4,128,4,128,...,4,0] — layers 0-1 uncompressed, then alternating CSA layers (4x KV compression) and HCA layers (128x KV compression). Uncompressed per-token KV = 512+64=576 elem/layer; apply per-layer compress ratio for effective cache.

variants.native_dtype: fp4 (MoE experts) + fp8 mixed (config torch_dtype=bfloat16; safetensors total 159.6 GB)

variants.name: DeepSeek-V4-Pro
variants.attention_type: linear/other — DeepSeek-V4 hybrid CSA+HCA (model_type deepseek_v4, NOT MLA): single shared KV head (num_key_value_heads=1, head_dim=512) + qk_rope_head_dim=64, with per-layer KV compression and DSA-style indexer (index_n_heads=64, index_head_dim=128, index_topk=1024); vendor claims ~10% of V3.2 KV cache at 1M ctx

swa.pattern_note: NOT Gemma-style interleaved SWA. config sliding_window=128 is the local uncompressed window inside compressed attention. compress_ratios (len 62 = 61 layers + 1 MTP head): [128,128,4,128,4,...,128,4,128,0] — layers 0-1 HCA (128x KV compression), then alternating CSA (4x) / HCA (128x), final 0 for MTP head. Uncompressed per-token KV = 512+64=576 elem/layer; apply per-layer compress ratio for effective cache.

corrections.claim: V4-Pro / V4-Flash uncompressed KV cost per token per layer (family_notes + both V4 rows: 'head_dim=512 plus qk_rope_head_dim=64 -> uncompressed cost ~576 elem/token/layer like MLA')
corrections.was: 576 elements/token/layer (512 + 64)
corrections.should_be: 512 elements/token/layer. In V4 the 64 RoPE dims are INSIDE the 512-dim shared KV vector (nope_head_dim = head_dim - rope_head_dim = 448; rope applied to kv[..., -64:]), unlike MLA where the 64 rope dims are cached in addition to the 512 latent. DeepSeek's reference code: wkv = Linear(dim, head_dim=512); kv_cache buffer width = head_dim = 512. Using 576 systematically overestimates V4 KV by 12.5%.
corrections.source: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro/raw/main/inference/model.py (Attention.__init__: self.nope_head_dim = args.head_dim - args.rope_head_dim; self.wkv = Linear(self.dim, self.head_dim); register_buffer kv_cache [..., kv_cache_size, self.head_dim]; forward: apply_rotary_emb(kv[..., -rd:], ...))

corrections.claim: V4 compress_ratios value 0 meaning (both V4 pattern_notes: '0 = uncompressed', 'layers 0-1 uncompressed' for Flash, 'final 0 for MTP head' implying full cache)
corrections.was: compress_ratio 0 = uncompressed full-length KV cache for that layer
corrections.should_be: compress_ratio 0 = PURE SLIDING-WINDOW layer caching only the last 128 tokens (window_size), essentially zero KV at long context. Reference code: kv_cache_size = window_size + (max_seq_len // compress_ratio if compress_ratio else 0), with comment 'disable YaRN and use base rope_theta in pure sliding-window attention' for ratio==0. Every layer's cache = 128-token window + (seq_len/ratio) compressed 512-dim entries; ratio-0 layers (Flash layers 0,1,42 and both models' MTP head) hold ONLY the 128-token window. Researcher's reading would add ~3 full 1M-token layers to V4-Flash (~1.7e9 extra elements); combined with the 576-vs-512 error it overestimates V4-Flash total KV at 1M ctx by roughly 75-80% (actual ~2.6e9 elements: 20 CSA layers x 250K x 512 + 20 HCA layers x 7.8K x 512 + 3 SWA layers x 128 x 512).
corrections.source: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro/raw/main/inference/model.py (Attention.__init__ line 'kv_cache_size = args.window_size + (args.max_seq_len // self.compress_ratio if self.compress_ratio else 0)' and the ratio-0 comment)

corrections.claim: V4-Pro compress_ratios terminal pattern (pattern_note '[128,128,4,128,4,...,128,4,128,0]')
corrections.was: array ends ...,128,4,128,0 (last transformer layer HCA 128x before MTP 0)
corrections.should_be: array ends ...,4,128,4,0 — the last transformer layer (layer 60) is CSA (4x), then 0 for the MTP head. Verified full 62-entry array from config.json: [128,128] then strictly alternating 4,128 for layers 2-59, layer 60 = 4, entry 61 = 0. (Lengths confirmed: Pro 62 = 61+1, Flash 44 = 43+1; Flash ending [...,128,4,0] matches researcher.)

corrections.claim: 671B-class FP8 weights disk/VRAM footprint (family_notes 'native FP8 ... ≈ 671 GB on disk')
corrections.should_be: 688.6 GB safetensors total for V3/V3-0324/R1/R1-0528/V3.1/V3.1-Terminus/Prover-V2-671B (684.53B params: ~680.6B FP8 @1B + ~3.9B BF16 @2B + F32 scales); 689.5 GB for V3.2/V3.2-Exp/Speciale (685.40B params). The '671 GB' figure conflates the headline param count with gigabytes and underestimates weights by ~18 GB (~2.6%).

corrections.claim: V4-Pro weights size (family_notes 'V4-Pro ~0.9 TB (not directly verified — index file not readable)')
corrections.was: ~0.9 TB, unverified
corrections.should_be: 864.7 GB safetensors total (verified via HF API blobs; dtype breakdown I8=786.4B packed-FP4 entries = ~1.573T FP4 expert params, consistent with the 1.6T headline). V4-Flash 159.6 GB confirmed exactly as stated.

corrections.claim: V4 'swa.global_layer_every: 2' field (both V4 rows)
corrections.was: global_layer_every = 2 (implies half the layers are global full-attention layers)
corrections.should_be: No global full-attention layers exist in V4; this field is fabricated/misleading. The alternation is CSA(4x-compressed) / HCA(128x-compressed), every layer also keeping a 128-token uncompressed local window. A calculator should drive purely off the compress_ratios array + window_size=128, not a global/local interleave parameter. (The accompanying pattern_note text says this correctly; the structured field contradicts it.)
corrections.source: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro/raw/main/inference/model.py (Attention/Compressor classes; no full-attention path, all layers windowed + optional compressed cache)

missing: V4 lightning-indexer cache detail: in V4 the Indexer exists ONLY on CSA (compress_ratio==4) layers (model.py: 'if self.compress_ratio == 4: self.indexer = Indexer(...) else None') and its cache holds max_seq_len/4 entries of index_head_dim=128 per CSA layer (register_buffer kv_cache [bsz, max_seq_len//4, 128]) — i.e. ~32 elements/token/CSA-layer, not the per-token 128-elem cache the V3.2 note implies; HCA and pure-SWA layers carry no indexer cache.

DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Base and DeepSeek-V4-Flash-Base (HF: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Base, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash-Base, created 2026-04-27): same architecture but 'FP8 Mixed' precision per the official model card table (no FP4 experts), so substantially larger weight footprints than the FP4+FP8 instruct releases — relevant if the calculator lists base models.

DeepSeek-Math-V2 (deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-Math-V2, 2025-11-27): model_type deepseek_v32, config-identical KV geometry to V3.2 (61L, kv_lora_rank 512, index_topk 2048, 163840 ctx), 685.4B params, 689.5 GB FP8 safetensors — a V3.2-architecture variant users may try to run.

DeepSeek-Prover-V2-671B (deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-Prover-V2-671B, 2025-04-30): model_type deepseek_v3, identical MLA geometry to V3/R1 (61L, kv_lora_rank 512, 163840 ctx), 684.5B params, 688.6 GB FP8 safetensors.
DeepSeek-R1-Zero (config-identical to DeepSeek-R1) — should be named in the dedup list alongside the other config-identical omissions.

V4 KV-cache dtype guidance: reference implementation stores the cache in BF16 with non-rope dims FP8-quantized (QAT) and rope dims kept BF16 ('kv could also use fp8 format' per code comment) — bytes-per-element for V4 KV should allow both 2-byte and ~1-byte serving modes.

Note for UI: DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Max / V4-Flash-Max are reasoning-effort MODES of the same checkpoints (per official model card), not separate models — calculator should not list them as distinct weights.
verification.summary: Re-verified every variant row against live HuggingFace config.json/API data on 2026-06-12. All raw table fields check out exactly: distill configs (1.5B 28L/12H/2KV, Qwen3-8B 36L/8KV/head_dim128, 14B 48L, 32B 64L, 70B 80L/8KV; params 1.78/8.19/14.77/32.76/70.55B; all 131072 ctx; Qwen2 distills use_sliding_window=false), MLA dims (kv_lora_rank 512, qk_rope 64, q_lora 1536, qk_nope/v_head 128, 61L/7168/128H, vestigial num_key_value_heads=128, YaRN 40x from 4096 -> 163840), V3.2 DSA fields (index 64/128/2048), and V4 existence/specs (V4-Pro 1.6T/49B, V4-Flash 284B/13B, 1M ctx via YaRN 16x from 65536, FP4 experts + FP8, hc_mult 4, hash layers 3, sqrtsoftplus, top-6, n_routed 384/256, index_topk 1024/512, Flash 159.6 GB exact). Config-identity claims confirmed (V3.2==Exp==Speciale byte-identical; R1==R1-0528 identical; V3 vs 0324 and V3.1 vs Terminus differ only in metadata keys). R2 confirmed nonexistent (absent from deepseek-ai org). The vendor '10% of V3.2 KV at 1M' claim is verbatim in the V4 model card. HOWEVER, DeepSeek's own reference code (V4-Pro repo inference/model.py) contradicts the researcher's V4 KV formula in two calculator-breaking ways: (1) uncompressed per-token cost is 512 elem/layer, not 576 — the 64 rope dims live inside head_dim=512; (2) compress_ratio 0 means a pure 128-token sliding-window layer (near-zero cache), not 'uncompressed' — per-layer cache is 128-window + seq/ratio compressed entries. Together these make the researcher's V4 method overestimate V4-Flash KV at 1M ctx by roughly 75-80%. Also corrected: 671B FP8 weights are 688.6-689.5 GB on disk (not '~671 GB'), V4-Pro weights verified at 864.7 GB, V4-Pro compress_ratios terminal pattern, and the spurious 'global_layer_every: 2' field. Verdict is major-corrections solely because the V4 KV-cache special-casing instructions — the part a calculator must implement from prose — are materially wrong; everything else is clean.

research.family_notes: NAMING REALITY CHECK: the K2 lineage as of 2026-06-12 is K2-Base/K2-Instruct (2025-07), K2-Instruct-0905 (2025-09), K2-Thinking (2025-11), Kimi-K2.5 (2026-01-01) and Kimi-K2.6 (2026-04-14, current flagship, GA 2026-04-20). 'Kimi K3' is unreleased rumor only (r/LocalLLaMA speculation of 3-4T params). K2.5 and K2.6 are NATIVE MULTIMODAL (architecture KimiK25ForConditionalGeneration = DeepSeek-V3-style text decoder + 400M MoonViT vision tower) but they ARE the current text/instruct flagships, not VL-only, so they are in scope; their text_config is byte-identical in all KV-relevant fields to K2-Thinking. ATTENTION/VRAM MATH: every K2-lineage model uses DeepSeek-V3-style MLA — ignore config's vestigial num_key_value_heads=64; KV cache per token per layer = kv_lora_rank(512) + qk_rope_head_dim(64) = 576 elements, so at bf16 cache = 1152 B/layer/token x 61 layers ~= 68.6 KiB/token (~17.6 GB at 256K context). MLA q/k head dim is 192 (qk_nope_head_dim 128 + qk_rope_head_dim 64), v_head_dim 128; hidden/heads = 7168/64 = 112 is NOT a real head dim, so head_dim is null for MLA entries. MoE shape (K2 line): 384 routed experts, top-8 + 1 shared expert, first layer dense (first_k_dense_replace=1), moe_intermediate_size 2048. Context is YaRN-extended (original_max_position_embeddings=4096, factor 32 -> 131072 for K2-Base/Instruct, factor 64 -> 262144 for 0905/Thinking/K2.5/K2.6). NATIVE DTYPES (load-bearing for weight VRAM): K2-Base/Instruct/0905 ship as FP8 block-quant (e4m3, 128x128 blocks, ~1.03 TB on disk; config torch_dtype bfloat16 is misleading); K2-Thinking/K2.5/K2.6 ship as native INT4 QAT (compressed-tensors pack-quantized, group_size 32, symmetric, WEIGHTS-ONLY and ROUTED-EXPERTS-ONLY — self_attn, shared_experts, dense mlp, lm_head, vision tower stay BF16 per the quantization_config ignore list; ~594-600 GB on disk, ~44B params remain BF16 per safetensors metadata). Realistic local deployment: official INT4 needs ~8xH200/B200 or KTransformers/llama.cpp CPU+GPU offload with ~600 GB RAM; unsloth dynamic GGUFs are the common local route (unsloth/Kimi-K2.6-GGUF 243K downloads, also Kimi-K2.5-GGUF, Kimi-K2-Thinking-GGUF, Kimi-K2-Instruct-GGUF; ~1.8-bit ~245 GB, Q4 ~380+ GB) plus mlx-community 4-bit for 512 GB Apple Silicon. KIMI-LINEAR special case: hybrid linear-attention model, 27 layers = 20 KDA (Kimi Delta Attention, gated-DeltaNet-style linear) + 7 full-attention MLA layers (full_attn_layers [4,8,12,16,20,24,27], 3:1 ratio); MLA layers are NoPE (mla_use_nope=true) but modeling_kimi.py confirms the cached kv_a_proj output is still kv_lora_rank+qk_rope = 576 elems/token/layer -> KV cache only ~7.9 KiB/token @bf16 across the 7 MLA layers, plus constant-size KDA recurrent/conv state (32 heads x head_dim 128, conv kernel 4) — Moonshot claims up to 75% KV-cache reduction; 1M-token context. Kimi-Dev-72B (dense, Qwen2.5-72B-based GQA coder) and Moonlight-16B-A3B (prev-gen 8K-context small MoE) are included as the only dense / small options in the family but are PREVIOUS generation (mid/early 2025).

corrections.claim: Kimi-Linear-48B-A3B-Instruct — attention_type note: 'KV cache exists ONLY on the 7 MLA layers at 576 elems/token/layer (code-verified)' and family_notes 'modeling_kimi.py confirms the cached kv_a_proj output is still kv_lora_rank+qk_rope = 576 elems/token/layer'
corrections.was: 576 elems/token/layer presented as verified from the HF reference code modeling_kimi.py
corrections.should_be: The 576/token/MLA-layer figure is correct for MLA-absorbed inference engines (vLLM/SGLang) and is consistent with Moonshot's ~75% KV-reduction claim (7/27 layers ≈ 26% of a full-MLA stack), so KEEP 576 in the calculator — but the cited justification is wrong: in modeling_kimi.py the KimiMLAAttention.forward calls past_key_values.update(key_states, value_states) AFTER kv_b_proj expansion, caching per-head K (q_head_dim 192) and V (v_head_dim 128) for all 32 heads = 32x(192+128) = 10,240 elems/token/layer in naive HF transformers inference. Calculator may want a footnote that naive transformers KV is ~17.8x larger.
corrections.source: https://huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-Linear-48B-A3B-Instruct/raw/main/modeling_kimi.py (lines ~388-414: key_shape uses qk_nope_head_dim+v_head_dim, k_rot.expand to all heads, then past_key_values.update(key_states, value_states))

corrections.claim: family_notes — unsloth GGUF sizes: '~1.8-bit ~245 GB, Q4 ~380+ GB'
corrections.was: Q4 ~380+ GB; 1.8-bit ~245 GB implied available across the family incl. Kimi-K2.6-GGUF
corrections.should_be: Q4-class GGUFs are 547-646 GB, not ~380 GB (UD-Q4_K_XL: K2-Instruct 587.1 GB, K2.6 583.7 GB, K2-Thinking 646.2 GB, K2.5 622.0 GB; IQ4_XS ~547 GB). ~380 GB corresponds to Q2-class quants (Q2_K ~373-374 GB, UD-Q2_K_XL 374.8-386.3 GB). The ~245 GB 1.8-bit (UD-TQ1_0) figure is correct but ONLY exists for K2-Instruct (243.6 GB), K2-Thinking (246.8 GB) and K2.5 (239.5 GB); unsloth/Kimi-K2.6-GGUF has NO 1.8-bit or IQ1/IQ2 quant — its smallest is UD-Q2_K_XL at 340.5 GB (plus ~1 GB mmproj for vision). This materially changes can-I-run-it verdicts for K2.6.

corrections.should_be: 2026-01-01 is only the HF repo creation date (createdAt 2026-01-01T06:06:03Z); the public open-source release was 2026-01-27. K2.6 dates check out (repo created 2026-04-14, GA 2026-04-20).

corrections.claim: family_notes — MLA KV cache '~17.6 GB at 256K context'
corrections.should_be: 1152 B/layer/token x 61 layers = 70,272 B/token (68.625 KiB ✓); x 262,144 tokens = 18.42 GB decimal (= 17.16 GiB). '17.6' matches neither unit; use 18.4 GB or 17.2 GiB.
corrections.source: Computed from verified config values: kv_lora_rank 512 + qk_rope_head_dim 64, num_hidden_layers 61, bf16 (https://huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2-Thinking/raw/main/config.json)

corrections.claim: K2-Thinking/K2.5/K2.6 — '~44B params remain BF16 per safetensors' / '~43.4B bf16 params' / 'bf16 ~43.9B params'
corrections.was: 43.4B (Thinking) / 43.9B (K2.5/K2.6) described as parameters remaining in BF16
corrections.should_be: BF16 element counts are exactly right (43,431,131,776 Thinking; 43,902,267,888 K2.5/K2.6 — diff 471M = MoonViT+projector, so '400M vision tower' is really ~0.47B added BF16), BUT ~31.7B of those BF16 elements are INT4 group-quantization scales (1,014,687,129,600 routed-expert int4 weights / group 32), not model weights; true unquantized weight params are ~11.7B (text) + 0.47B (vision). VRAM math is unaffected (BF16 tensors still occupy ~86.9-87.8 GB), so this is a description fix only. Total param figures 1026.5B/1026.9B remain correct once scales are excluded.

corrections.claim: Kimi-K2.6 — native_dtype '~600 GB weights'
corrections.should_be: 595.3 GB (usedStorage 595,263,427,116 bytes); K2.5 is 595.2 GB; K2-Thinking 594.3 GB. Rounding to ~595 GB is more accurate; '~594-600 GB' range in family_notes is acceptable.

missing: Kimi-VL-A3B-Instruct (moonshotai/Kimi-VL-A3B-Instruct, 268K downloads — the most-downloaded locally-runnable Moonshot model after the GGUFs): 16.41B total BF16 params (~3B active incl. ~0.4B MoonViT), Moonlight-style MLA text decoder (27 layers, hidden 2048, 16 heads, kv_lora_rank 512 + qk_rope 64 → same 576-elem MLA cache, 64 experts top-6 + 2 shared) but with max_position_embeddings 131072 (vs Moonlight's 8192). Major omission for a VRAM calculator since it fits on a single 24-48 GB GPU. Source: https://huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-VL-A3B-Instruct/raw/main/config.json

Kimi-VL-A3B-Thinking and Kimi-VL-A3B-Thinking-2506 (moonshotai/Kimi-VL-A3B-Thinking, 123K downloads; -2506 refresh 2025-06): same 16.4B A3B MLA architecture, reasoning variants. Source: https://huggingface.co/api/models?author=moonshotai

Kimi-Audio-7B / Kimi-Audio-7B-Instruct (moonshotai/Kimi-Audio-7B-Instruct, 80K downloads): 9.77B total params, Qwen2.5-7B-based audio-in/audio-out LLM; the only ~10 GB-class model in the family. Source: https://huggingface.co/api/models/moonshotai/Kimi-Audio-7B-Instruct
Kimi-Linear-48B-A3B-Base and Moonlight-16B-A3B (base, non-instruct variants; same VRAM profile as their Instruct entries). Source: https://huggingface.co/api/models?author=moonshotai
MoonViT-SO-400M (standalone vision encoder repo, confirms the ~400M vision-tower figure). Source: https://huggingface.co/moonshotai/MoonViT-SO-400M
llama.cpp vision for K2.5/K2.6 GGUF requires a separate mmproj file (~1.0 GB F16) on top of quant sizes. Source: https://huggingface.co/api/models/unsloth/Kimi-K2.6-GGUF/tree/main?recursive=true

Refinement (not a new variant): 'Kimi K3' remains unreleased as of 2026-06-12 — researcher is correct — but it was officially teased by Moonshot on 2026-03-28 (secondary press reports ~2.5T params, Q3 2026 target; prediction markets only), so it is slightly more than 'r/LocalLLaMA speculation'. Sources: https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/04/20/... press, https://polymarket.com/event/kimi-k3-released-by, https://news.aibase.com/news/27577
verification.summary: Re-verified all 9 variants directly against HuggingFace config.json files and the HF API (safetensors param/dtype breakdowns, storage sizes, createdAt), plus unsloth GGUF repo file listings and web press for post-cutoff releases. The researcher's core architectural data is excellent: every num_layers / hidden_size / MLA dim (kv_lora_rank 512, qk_rope 64, qk_nope 128, v_head 128, q_lora 1536-or-null) / MoE shape (384 top-8+1, 256 top-8+1, 64 top-6+2) / YaRN factor / max_context / quantization_config (fp8 e4m3 128x128 vs int4 group-32 compressed-tensors with exact ignore lists) matches primary sources, as do all params_total_b values (1026.47B, 1026.9B, 49.12B, 15.96B, 72.71B), the 594-595 GB int4 / 1.03 TB fp8 disk sizes, K2.5/K2.6 existence and K2.6 dates, Kimi-Dev's use_sliding_window=false, Kimi-Linear's full_attn_layers [4,8,12,16,20,24,27] and 1M context, and the unreleased status of 'Kimi K3'. Found 4 substantive issues: (1) the unsloth GGUF guidance 'Q4 ~380+ GB' is wrong (Q4-class is 547-646 GB; ~380 GB is Q2-class) and K2.6 has no 1.8-bit quant at all (smallest 340.5 GB) — this directly affects can-I-run-it verdicts; (2) the Kimi-Linear 576-elem KV figure is right for vLLM-style absorbed MLA but the 'code-verified from modeling_kimi.py' justification is false — the HF reference code caches expanded per-head K/V (10,240 elems/token/MLA-layer); (3) '~17.6 GB at 256K' should be 18.4 GB / 17.2 GiB; (4) the '~44B BF16 params' in the int4 models include ~31.7B quantization scales (true BF16 weights ~12B; VRAM bytes unchanged). K2.5's public release date was 2026-01-27, not the 2026-01-01 repo-creation date. Biggest gap: the family list omits Kimi-VL-A3B (3 repos, 16.4B A3B MLA VLM, 128K ctx — the family's main single-GPU option), Kimi-Audio-7B(-Instruct), and the Base variants of Kimi-Linear and Moonlight.

research.family_notes: Naming reality check: the CURRENT generation as of June 2026 is the 'Nemotron 3' family (hybrid Mamba2-Transformer MoE): Nano-30B-A3B (released 2025-12-15), Super-120B-A12B (2026-03-11, GTC), Ultra-550B-A55B (2026-06-04, Computex), plus a dense hybrid Nano-4B (Elastic-compressed from Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2). 'Nemotron-H' is the architecture/research name — ALL hybrid Nemotron models (Nemotron-H, Nano v2, Nemotron 3) use HF model_type 'nemotron_h' / NemotronHForCausalLM. NVIDIA suffixes repos by precision (-BF16/-FP8/-NVFP4); the BF16 repo is the canonical weights (Super and Ultra were pretrained with native NVFP4 per NVIDIA, and the NVFP4 repos quantize experts to NVFP4 + Mamba projections to FP8). VRAM-critical KV math: in all hybrid models the KV cache exists ONLY for the few attention layers, not num_hidden_layers — use the attention-layer count given per variant (e.g. Ultra-550B: 12 attention layers of 108 total; per-token KV = 2 * 12 layers * 2 kv_heads * 128 head_dim * dtype_bytes ≈ 12 KB/token at FP16 — tiny). Mamba2 layers instead hold a constant-size per-sequence SSM state (independent of context length); Nemotron 3 configs set mamba_ssm_cache_dtype=float32. Attention layers are plain global GQA — sliding_window is null everywhere, no interleaved SWA, no MLA anywhere in the family. Nemotron 3 Super/Ultra additionally have a 1-layer multi-token-prediction (MTP) module (one extra attention+MoE pair, mtp pattern '*E') used for speculative decoding — adds one more KV-cached attention layer only if MTP is enabled. Nemotron 3 configs ship max_position_embeddings=262144 but model cards state up to 1M tokens supported (256K is the HF default 'due to higher VRAM requirements'); Nano-4B is capped at 262K. The Llama-Nemotron line (Super-49B-v1.5, Ultra-253B-v1, DeciLM/Puzzle-NAS architecture) is the previous generation (mid-2025) but still widely served (49B-v1.5 ~787K monthly downloads); its NAS quirk: many blocks have attention.no_op=true (no attention, no KV) — Super-49B has real attention/KV in only 49 of 80 blocks, Ultra-253B in only 64 of 162 blocks; all real attention layers are uniform GQA (8 KV heads, head_dim 128, no windowed layers, replace_with_linear=false everywhere in these two). Excluded per scope: Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B (any-to-any multimodal), Nano-12B-v2-VL, Parse/embed/rerank/safety models, and the older Nemotron-H-8B/47B/56B research line (superseded by Nano v2 / Nemotron 3).
