support_hybrid_kv_cache=True lets Gemma3/4 / GPT-OSS share DRAM tensors across full-attention and sliding-window layer groups instead of allocating full-size KV for every layer; prevents OOM (e.g. Gemma3-27B on T3K). Currently partly disabled (_HYBRID_KV_CACHE_GROUPS_ENABLED=False) so all layers emit FullAttentionSpec.
Saves approximately the full model size in host memory; tt-transformers README states ~140GB+ for a 70B model. This is host RAM, not device DRAM.

frameworks.notes: Repo: github.com/tenstorrent/vllm (dev branch), serving plugin at plugins/vllm-tt-plugin. Decode max batch = 32 users (hardware decode limit); some configs run max_num_seqs 128/256 (Llama-8B on Galaxy/P150X8). Top-k logprobs clamped to 20 (device computes top-32). On-device sampling gated by model_capabilities. Supported LLM architectures (TT-prefixed): Llama (TTLlamaForCausalLM), Qwen2.5/Qwen3/QwQ, Mistral/Mistral3, Gemma3/Gemma4, DeepSeek-V3/R1, GPT-OSS, plus vision (Mllama, Qwen2.5-VL, Mistral3).

frameworks.name: tt-transformers (model library in tt-metal; backend that actually allocates KV/weights)
frameworks.kv_strategy: Provides the TT-NN paged KV cache implementation used by tt-vllm: allocate_vllm_kv_cache builds per-layer torch.zeros(kv_cache_shape) tensors moved to device, with shape (num_blocks, num_kv_heads, block_size, head_size). Prefill fills via ttnn.experimental.paged_fill_cache; decode updates via ttnn.experimental.paged_update_cache with position indices; attention via paged_scaled_dot_product_attention_decode. Page tables can be per-layer (page_tables_per_layer) for hybrid models or a single shared page table (legacy path). Decode batch capped at 32 users.
frameworks.default_kv_dtype: ttnn.bfloat8_b (explicit fallback in code: 'kv_cache_dtype = ttnn.bfloat8_b if kv_cache_dtype is None'). Governed by DecodersPrecision/ModelOptimizations: default profile sets KV_CACHE = BFP8.
kv_dtype_options: bfloat16 / BF16 (accuracy profile for sensitive models)

memory_overhead.description: ModelOptimizations has two named tiers: performance (default for most generators via DecodersPrecision.performance) and accuracy. Default precision map = BFP8 everywhere with HIFI2 math fidelity, activations follow input dtype (usually BF16). MLP linears use BFP8 with FP16 accumulation 'to save L1'. Weights in DRAM; activation tensors in L1 (decode) or DRAM (prefill). No single fixed GB overhead — dominated by weights + KV + trace region set by the serving layer.
memory_overhead.activation_model: Decode activations resident in L1 SRAM (1.5 MB/Tensix core); prefill activations in DRAM, tiled (reshape at seq_len>=1024 for FF, >2048 for attention). MLP uses FP16 accumulation to reduce L1 pressure.

frameworks.preallocates: get_max_tokens_all_users() returns the fixed token budget the serving layer uses to size KV blocks (default FALLBACK 131072; per-model/device overrides like 32768 / 65536). This is the source of truth for KV sizing — there is no runtime profiling.
frameworks.hardware_support: Wormhole (N150, N300, T3K, TG/Galaxy) and Blackhole (P100, P150, P300 and multi-card P150X4/X8, P300X2). is_wormhole_b0() branches select per-arch token budgets.

savings_techniques.technique: BFP8 default precision with HIFI2 + FP16 MLP accumulation
savings_techniques.saving: All weights and KV at ~1.0625 B/elem (BFP8) vs 2 B (BF16) ~= 1.88x model+KV memory reduction (rough); FP16 accumulation in MLP linears reduces L1 working-set.

savings_techniques.technique: BFP4 MLP weights (FF1/FF3) in performance profile
savings_techniques.saving: FF1/FF3 projection weights at ~0.5625 B/param (BFP4) vs 2 B (BF16) ~= 3.56x reduction on those tensors; applied to all models in performance mode and to all weights of >70B models even in accuracy mode (deemed insensitive).

Independent precision for WQKV, WO, FF1_FF3, FF2, KV_CACHE, ACTIVATION lets memory be trimmed where accuracy permits (e.g. WQKV->BFP8 when a model 'runs out of DRAM for weight fetching under accuracy settings').

frameworks.notes: DecodersPrecision.from_string('performance'|'accuracy'); PrecisionSetting enum = {BFP4='bfp4', BFP8='bfp8', BF16}. Default generator dtype passed as ttnn.bfloat8_b. This library is consumed by both the tt-vllm plugin and standalone tt-metal demos.

frameworks.name: tt-inference-server (orchestration layer wrapping tt-vllm)
frameworks.kv_strategy: Does not implement KV cache itself; it launches the tt-vllm server with per-model/per-device vllm_args from release_model_spec.json. These pin KV-relevant params: block_size (always '64' for LLMs, '32' for DeepSeek-R1), max_model_len, max_num_seqs (= max concurrency), max_num_batched_tokens (currently hardcoded, usually == max_model_len per issue #2491), and override_tt_config (trace_region_size, fabric_config, l1_small_size, worker_l1_size, sample_on_device_mode, dispatch_core_axis).
frameworks.default_kv_dtype: Inherits tt-vllm/tt-transformers default (bfloat8_b). Not separately configurable here beyond passing additional_config to the engine.

memory_overhead.description: Specifies host requirements per model (min_ram_gb, min_disk_gb): e.g. Llama-3.2-1B 2.5GB RAM/22GB disk; Llama-3.1-8B 20GB/36GB; Llama-3.3-70B / 3.1-70B 175GB/160GB; Llama-3.2-90B-Vision 225GB/200GB; Gemma3-27B 67.5GB/74GB; gpt-oss-20b 50GB RAM/60GB disk. Docs state deploying recommended models needs >=360GB free disk. Device-side trace_region_size is passed through override_tt_config (30MB-384MB depending on model/board).
memory_overhead.activation_model: Delegated to tt-vllm/tt-transformers. The server's job is to map (model, device) -> validated vllm_args + env_vars and run the Docker image (ghcr.io/tenstorrent/tt-inference-server/vllm-tt-metal-src-release-...).

frameworks.preallocates: No fractional VRAM preallocation. Effective device memory = weights + KV sized to max_model_len/max_concurrency + trace_region_size, as configured per row. max_context per device class is the binding limit (see notes).
frameworks.hardware_support: Device classes in release_model_spec.json (release 0.15.0): N150, N300, N150X4, T3K, GALAXY, GALAXY_T3K, DUAL_GALAXY, QUAD_GALAXY (Wormhole); P100, P150, P300, P150X4, P150X8, P300X2 (Blackhole); plus GPU reference. Also a v2 server (tt-inference-server-v2) and tt-media-server for non-LLM.

savings_techniques.saving: Caps max_model_len to fit KV in device DRAM: most LLMs 131072; Llama-3.1-8B on N150 reduced to 65536 (vs 131072 on N300/T3K); Qwen3-8B 40960 all devices; gpt-oss 16384; DeepSeek-R1 2048-32768; Qwen2.5-VL-3B/7B 32768. Smaller context => proportionally smaller KV cache.

Most LLMs max_concurrency 32; raised to 128/256 on multi-card (Llama-8B P150X8=256, GALAXY=128) or lowered to 1 (gpt-oss, DeepSeek on single Galaxy) to fit KV/activation memory. Lower concurrency => fewer KV blocks reserved.
Sets DRAM trace buffer exactly per board (e.g. 30MB on T3K vs 384MB/268MB/215MB on dense multi-chip boards), avoiding over-reservation of device DRAM.

frameworks.notes: max_num_batched_tokens currently hardcoded == max context; tracked for per-device tuning in issue #2491. Status flags: COMPLETE / FUNCTIONAL / EXPERIMENTAL per (model,device). block_size 64 universal for LLMs. release_version 0.15.0, 62 model specs total (LLM + VLM + media/diffusion + embedding).

frameworks.name: TT-Studio (GUI deployment frontend)
frameworks.kv_strategy: No independent KV cache management. TT-Studio is a containerized GUI/control plane that deploys and manages model backends which are tt-inference-server Docker images running tt-vllm. All KV cache, paging, and precision behavior is inherited from tt-vllm/tt-transformers. It exposes model selection, deployment, and a chat/playground UI.

memory_overhead.description: TT-Studio's own footprint is host-side (Docker, web UI, backend orchestration); device memory is entirely determined by the deployed tt-inference-server model image and its vllm_args. No separate device VRAM accounting.
memory_overhead.activation_model: Delegated to the underlying tt-vllm backend.

frameworks.preallocates: No. Memory semantics are exactly those of the tt-vllm backend it launches.
frameworks.hardware_support: Same Wormhole/Blackhole device classes supported by tt-inference-server (n150/n300/T3K/Galaxy, p100/p150/p300).

savings_techniques.saving: No additional VRAM-saving mechanism beyond what the deployed tt-vllm backend provides (paged attention, BFP8 KV, BFP4/BFP8 weights, per-device context/concurrency caps).

frameworks.notes: TT-Studio is a deployment/management UI layer; for a VRAM calculator it should be treated as a pass-through to tt-inference-server + tt-vllm. Do not model separate device memory for it.

corrections.claim: tt-inference-server savings 'Concurrency tuning': 'raised to 128/256 on multi-card (Llama-8B P150X8=256, GALAXY=128)'; also tt-vllm notes 'some configs run max_num_seqs 128/256 (Llama-8B on Galaxy/P150X8)'
corrections.was: Llama-3.1-8B uses max_num_seqs 256 on P150X8 and 128 on GALAXY
corrections.should_be: In release_model_spec.json 0.15.0, Llama-3.1-8B's only elevated concurrency is max_num_seqs=128 on P150X4 (its P150X8 and GALAXY entries are both 32). The single max_num_seqs=256 in the whole spec belongs to DeepSeek-R1-0528 on GALAXY, NOT Llama-8B. The other max_num_seqs=128 entries are Qwen3-8B, QwQ-32B, and Qwen2.5-72B(-Instruct) on GALAXY. This matters because the KV block count = ceil((max_tokens_all_users + block_size*max_num_seqs)/block_size), so a wrong max_num_seqs directly skews the computed KV size.

corrections.claim: tt-inference-server savings 'Concurrency tuning': '...or lowered to 1 (gpt-oss, DeepSeek on single Galaxy) to fit KV/activation memory'
corrections.was: gpt-oss and DeepSeek are lowered to max_num_seqs=1 on a single Galaxy
corrections.should_be: max_num_seqs=1 for gpt-oss-20b applies on T3K and GALAXY_T3K, and for gpt-oss-120b on T3K; on a single GALAXY gpt-oss-20b and gpt-oss-120b both run max_num_seqs=32 (mml 131072). DeepSeek never uses max_num_seqs=1 anywhere: DeepSeek-R1-0528 is 256 on GALAXY and 32 on DUAL_GALAXY/QUAD_GALAXY; DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B is 8 (GALAXY) / 32 (T3K, P150X4/8, P300X2). The only other LLMs at max_num_seqs=1 are Llama-3.2-3B and Qwen3-4B on N150/N300 (remaining mns=1 entries are diffusion/embedding/vision/whisper models).

corrections.claim: tt-vllm kv_strategy: 'Default block_size = 64 ... DeepSeek-R1 uses 32'
corrections.was: DeepSeek-R1 uses block_size 32 (stated generically)
corrections.should_be: block_size '32' appears in exactly 2 entries, both DeepSeek-R1-0528 on DUAL_GALAXY and QUAD_GALAXY. DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B and DeepSeek-R1-0528 on single GALAXY do not pin block_size 32 (default 64). The 'DeepSeek-R1 uses 32' shorthand is only correct for the R1-0528 multi-Galaxy configs, not the Distill variants.

missing: Concurrency reduction for 70B-class and 27B models on a single GALAXY: Llama-3.3-70B, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B, Gemma-3-27b-it and medgemma-27b-it all run max_num_seqs=8 on GALAXY (vs 32 on T3K). The research's blanket 'most LLMs max_concurrency 32' misses this per-device down-tuning, which reduces reserved KV blocks on Galaxy.
max_num_seqs=16 is used for Llama-3.2-11B-Vision(-Instruct) on N300/T3K (between the '1' and '32' extremes the research lists).

The 'decode max batch = 32 (hardware decode limit)' is per data-parallel replica; with tt_data_parallel>1 on multi-card meshes the effective max_num_seqs scales above 32 (e.g. 128/256), which is why those values appear. Worth stating so the calculator does not treat 32 as a hard global cap.
verification.summary: The TT-stack research is largely accurate and well-sourced. I independently re-fetched the primary sources (Tenstorrent hardware spec pages, tt-inference-server/release_model_spec.json @0.15.0, tt-metal models/tt_transformers/tt/generator_vllm.py + model_config.py, and the vllm-tt-plugin worker.py/platform.py) and confirmed the load-bearing numbers a VRAM calculator depends on: device DRAM caps (n150 12GB, n300 24GB, p100a 28GB, p150a/b 32GB, p300 64GB); release_version 0.15.0 with 62 specs and 15 device classes; default KV dtype bfloat8_b (verbatim fallback in generator_vllm.py); FALLBACK_MAX_TOKENS_ALL_USERS=131072 plus every per-model token-budget override (Llama-8B/Qwen3-8B N150->32768, Mistral-7B N150->65536, Llama-90B T3K->65536, DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-14B/Qwen2.5-14B N300->65536); the num_gpu_blocks_override 'no profiling' path and the +block_size*max_num_seqs worst-case heuristic; chunked-prefill force-disable and prefix-caching gating; all spot-checked trace_region_size values (52/70/117/50MB for Llama-8B; 215MB/268435456/402653184 for Llama-70B; 184915840 for Qwen3-32B Galaxy); all host min_ram_gb/min_disk_gb (1B 2.5/22, 8B 20/36, 70B 175/160, 90B-Vision 225/200, Gemma3-27B 67.5/74, gpt-oss-20b 50/60); context caps; and the block-float bytes-per-element math (bfp8 1.0625, bfp4 0.5625, both correct for 16-element blocks). The only real errors are in the max_num_seqs (concurrency) narrative: Llama-3.1-8B's elevated concurrency is 128 on P150X4 only (P150X8/GALAXY are 32), the lone 256 belongs to DeepSeek-R1-0528 on GALAXY, and DeepSeek is never lowered to max_num_seqs=1 (gpt-oss=1 is on T3K/GALAXY_T3K, not single Galaxy where it is 32). These misattributions affect computed KV size and should be corrected; the block_size-32 'DeepSeek-R1' shorthand is also imprecise (only R1-0528 on DUAL/QUAD_GALAXY). Verdict: minor-corrections.

research.findings:
# VRAM Calculator Math Module — Formulas (Topic 1: Weights + KV Cache)

All formulas verified against primary sources (llama.cpp source/static_asserts, vLLM v1 source, HF configs, papers) on 2026-06-12. Worked examples are checked against published numbers. GB = 1e9 bytes, GiB = 2^30 bytes — llama.cpp reports GiB, HF reports GB; the calculator must pick one and label it.

## 1(a). Weight memory

### Master formula

```
weight_bytes = Σ_over_tensor_groups ( params_in_group × bytes_per_weight(format_of_group) )
```

Simplified single-format form (good to ~1-3% for most checkpoints):

```
weight_bytes = total_params × (effective_bpw / 8)
```

The "effective_bpw" must include quantization-metadata overhead (group scales/zeros) and, for whole-checkpoint estimates, the higher-precision tensors (embeddings, output head, norms).

### Unquantized dtypes (exact)

```
FP32 = 4 bytes/param      FP16 / BF16 = 2 bytes/param
FP8 (E4M3/E5M2) = 1 byte/param  (+ scale overhead, see below)
INT8 = 1 byte/param       (+ per-channel scale, negligible)
```
Source: EleutherAI transformer-math ("memory_model = (2 bytes/param)·(No. params)" for fp16). EleutherAI/HF-accelerate heuristic for total *inference* footprint: `Total_inference ≈ 1.2 × model_memory` ("in our experience this overhead is ≤ 20%"); accelerate's estimator itself is pure `params × dtype_bytes`.

FP8 scale overhead: per-tensor scale ≈ 0 bpw; DeepSeek-style 128×128 block-wise FP32 scales add `32/(128×128) ≈ 0.002 bpw` — negligible. The *whole-checkpoint* effective bpw is dominated by non-FP8 tensors: DeepSeek-V3 (671B, FP8 weights + BF16 norms/embeddings + FP32 scales) = 688.59 GB on HF → **8.21 effective bpw whole-model**.

### GPTQ / AWQ k-bit with group size g (exact formula, validated)

Both store, per group of `g` weights along the input dim: one FP16 scale (16 bits) and one packed k-bit zero-point:

```
effective_bpw = k + (16 + k) / g
INT4 g=128: 4 + 20/128 = 4.15625 bpw      INT4 g=64: 4.3125     INT4 g=32: 4.625
INT8 g=128: 8 + 24/128 = 8.1875 bpw       (AutoGPTQ int8 zeros packed 4/word → use 8 + 20/128 = 8.156 if zeros stay 4-bit-equivalent; mark as estimate)
GPTQ act-order adds g_idx: +32/out_features bpw  (<0.01, negligible)
```

Pack layout (AutoAWQ gemm.py): `qweight: (in, out/8) int32`, `qzeros: (in/g, out/8) int32`, `scales: (in/g, out) fp16` — 8 nibbles per int32, confirming the formula.

**Non-quantized tensors:** GPTQ/AWQ leave `embed_tokens` and `lm_head` (and norms) in FP16:

```
ckpt_bytes ≈ linear_params × bpw/8 + (embed + head + norm)_params × 2
```

**Validation (Llama-3.1-8B, 8.03B total; embed=head=128256×4096=0.525B each, untied):**
linear ≈ 6.98B × 4.15625/8 + 1.05B × 2 = **5.727 GB predicted** vs actual HF checkpoints: hugging-quants GPTQ-INT4 = **5.736 GB** (+0.15%, the g_idx), AWQ-INT4 = **5.728 GB** (+0.01%). 

### bitsandbytes

```
NF4 / FP4, blocksize 64, fp32 absmax:        4 + 32/64        = 4.5 bpw
NF4 + double quantization (QLoRA default):   4 + 8/64 + 32/(64·256) ≈ 4.127 bpw
   (QLoRA paper: DQ "saves ~0.373 bits/param")
LLM.int8(): ≈ 8 bpw + fp16 outlier columns (~0.1% of weights) — estimate ~8.04 bpw
```

### GGUF ladder — two tables the calculator needs

**(i) Theoretical per-block bpw — EXACT, derived from `ggml-common.h` static_asserts (I parsed the current master file):**

| type | bytes/block | block | bpw |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q4_0 | 18/32 | 32 | 4.5 |
| Q4_1 | 20/32 | 32 | 5.0 |
| Q5_0 | 22/32 | 32 | 5.5 |
| Q5_1 | 24/32 | 32 | 6.0 |
| Q8_0 | 34/32 | 32 | 8.5 |
| Q2_K | 84/256 | 256 | 2.625 |
| Q3_K | 110/256 | 256 | 3.4375 |
| Q4_K | 144/256 | 256 | 4.5 |
| Q5_K | 176/256 | 256 | 5.5 |
| Q6_K | 210/256 | 256 | 6.5625 |
| IQ1_S | 50/256 | 256 | 1.5625 |
| IQ1_M | 56/256 | 256 | 1.75 |
| IQ2_XXS | 66/256 | 256 | 2.0625 |
| IQ2_XS | 74/256 | 256 | 2.3125 |
| IQ2_S | 82/256 | 256 | 2.5625 |
| IQ3_XXS | 98/256 | 256 | 3.0625 |
| IQ3_S | 110/256 | 256 | 3.4375 |
| IQ4_NL | 18/32 | 32 | 4.5 |
| IQ4_XS | 136/256 | 256 | 4.25 |
| MXFP4 | 17/32 | 32 | 4.25 |
| NVFP4 | 36/64 | 64 | 4.5 |
| TQ1_0 | 54/256 | 256 | 1.6875 |
| TQ2_0 | 66/256 | 256 | 2.0625 |

(Note: PR #1684's text says Q2_K = 2.5625 bpw; the *current* struct is 84 B = 2.625 bpw — use the current value.)

**(ii) Measured whole-model effective bpw (llama.cpp `tools/quantize/README.md`, benchmarked on Llama-3.1-8B) — this is what file sizes actually come out to, because quant "mixes" upgrade sensitive tensors:**

| ftype | eff. bpw | size (8B model) | | ftype | eff. bpw | size |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IQ1_S | 2.0042 | 1.87 GiB | | Q3_K_M | 3.9960 | 3.74 GiB |
| IQ1_M | 2.1460 | 2.01 GiB | | Q3_K_L | 4.2979 | 4.02 GiB |
| IQ2_XXS | 2.3824 | 2.23 GiB | | Q4_K_S | 4.6672 | 4.36 GiB |
| IQ2_XS | 2.5882 | 2.42 GiB | | **Q4_K_M** | **4.8944** | 4.58 GiB |
| IQ2_S | 2.7403 | 2.56 GiB | | Q5_K_S | 5.5704 | 5.21 GiB |
| IQ2_M | 2.9294 | 2.74 GiB | | Q5_K_M | 5.7036 | 5.33 GiB |
| IQ3_XXS | 3.2548 | 3.04 GiB | | Q6_K | 6.5633 | 6.14 GiB |
| IQ3_XS | 3.4977 | 3.27 GiB | | Q8_0 | 8.5008 | 7.95 GiB |
| IQ3_S | 3.6606 | 3.42 GiB | | F16 | 16.0005 | 14.96 GiB |
| IQ3_M | 3.7628 | 3.52 GiB | | Q2_K | 3.1593 | 2.95 GiB |
| Q2_K_S | 2.9697 | 2.78 GiB | | Q3_K_S | 3.6429 | 3.41 GiB |

Calculator rule: `gguf_bytes = params × measured_bpw / 8`. Validation: 8.0303e9 × 16.0005/8 = 14.96 GiB ✓, × 4.8944 = 4.58 GiB ✓, × 8.5008 = 7.95 GiB ✓ (all match README exactly). Mark measured bpw as **model-family estimates (±2-5%)**: the gap vs theoretical bpw (e.g. IQ1_S 2.00 vs 1.5625; Q4_K_M 4.89 vs 4.5) comes from the mix rules and varies with embed/head share of total params (small models and MoE differ most).

**Non-quantized-tensor mix rules (current `src/llama-quant.cpp`):**
- `output.weight` (and tied `token_embd`): Q6_K by default; Q8_0 if cols not divisible by 256 or for MXFP4_MOE ftype; Q5_K for IQ1/IQ2/IQ3_XXS family. `--output-tensor-type` / `--leave-output-tensor` override.
- `token_embd.weight` (untied): base type, except low-bit ftypes upgrade it (IQ2_XXS/XS/IQ1_* → Q2_K; IQ2_S/M and IQ3_XXS → IQ3_S; ternary → Q4_K).
- `attn_v.weight` upgraded (e.g. Q2_K→Q4_K when GQA≥4); first 1/8 of `ffn_down` layers upgraded in _M mixes (per PR #1684: attn_v, attn_output, ffn_down get higher bits in M/L mixes).
- `--pure` disables mixes → pure theoretical bpw.

**MXFP4 class (OCP Microscaling v1.0):** E2M1 4-bit elements, 32-element blocks, one shared E8M0 8-bit scale → **4.25 bpw exact** (ggml `block_mxfp4` = 17 B/32 w confirms). gpt-oss models post-trained with MoE weights in MXFP4 ("4.25 bits per parameter", model card); everything else BF16. gpt-oss-20b HF checkpoint = **13.76 GB** for 20.9B params → 5.27 effective bpw whole-model (>90% of params are MoE) — matches the "runs in 16 GB" claim. NVFP4 (Nvidia): 16-element sub-blocks with FP8-E4M3 scales → 4.5 bpw + per-tensor fp32 scale.

## 1(b). KV cache

### Standard MHA / GQA / MQA

```
kv_bytes_per_token = 2 × num_layers × num_kv_heads × head_dim × dtype_bytes
kv_total = kv_bytes_per_token × context_tokens × batch_size
```
- Factor 2 = K and V. Use `num_key_value_heads` (GQA: < num_attention_heads; MQA: 1; MHA: = num_attention_heads). `head_dim = hidden_size / num_attention_heads` unless config sets `head_dim` explicitly.
- This is exactly vLLM v1's `FullAttentionSpec.page_size_bytes = 2 * block_size * num_kv_heads * head_size * dtype_size`, and kipply's `2·2·n_layers·n_heads·d_head` (second 2 = fp16 bytes).
- KV dtype bytes: fp16/bf16 = 2, fp8 = 1, llama.cpp `--cache-type q8_0` = 8.5/8 ≈ 1.0625 B/elem, `q4_0` = 4.5/8 = 0.5625 B/elem.

**Worked examples (verified):**
- Qwen3-8B (36 L, 8 kv heads, head_dim 128, bf16): 2×36×8×128×2 = 147,456 B = **144 KiB/token** — matches Raschka's published table exactly.
- Llama-3.1-70B (80 L, 8 kv, 128): **320 KiB/token** → 40.0 GiB at 131,072 ctx.
- Llama-3.1-8B (32 L, 8 kv, 128): **128 KiB/token** → 16.0 GiB at 128K.
- kipply's 52B example (MHA, 64 L, d_model 8192): 4×64×8192 ≈ 2.1 MB/token ✓.

### MLA (DeepSeek V2/V3/R1, Kimi-K2)

Only the compressed latent + decoupled RoPE key are cached — there is no separate V cache, so **no factor 2**:

```
kv_bytes_per_token = num_layers × (kv_lora_rank + qk_rope_head_dim) × dtype_bytes
```
vLLM v1 `MLAAttentionSpec` drops the coefficient 2 and uses one "head" of size `kv_lora_rank + qk_rope_head_dim`.

**Worked example (DeepSeek-V3 config.json: 61 layers, kv_lora_rank=512, qk_rope_head_dim=64):**
(512+64) × 61 × 2 B = 70,272 B = **68.6 KiB/token** (matches Raschka's published 68.6 KiB) → 8.58 GiB at 128K, batch 1. Equivalent to GQA with only 2.25 groups (576/(2×128)), per the DeepSeek-V2 paper framing.

### Interleaved sliding-window (Gemma 2/3, gpt-oss, Mistral-style SWA)

Per-token-per-layer cost is the same as GQA; what changes is how many tokens each layer retains:

```
kv_total = Σ_layers [ 2 × num_kv_heads × head_dim × dtype_bytes × tokens(layer) ]
tokens(full_attention layer)    = min(ctx, max_position_embeddings)
tokens(sliding_attention layer) = min(ctx, sliding_window)
⇒ kv_total = per_tok_layer × ( n_global×min(ctx,max_ctx) + n_local×min(ctx,W) )
```
Layer split from config: Gemma-3 `sliding_window_pattern: 6` → every 6th layer global (5:1 local:global); gpt-oss `layer_types` alternates sliding/full (1:1). vLLM allocates `min(sliding_window - 1 + batch_tokens, max_model_len)` tokens for SWA layers (`SlidingWindowSpec`) — the `W-1` vs `W` distinction is noise; use `min(ctx, W)`.

**Worked examples:**
- Gemma-3-27B (62 L, 16 kv, head_dim 128, W=1024, pattern 6 → 10 global + 52 local; per-layer-token = 8 KiB): at 131,072 ctx bf16 → 10×131072×8KiB + 52×1024×8KiB = **10.41 GiB**, vs 62.0 GiB if all-global = **6.0× reduction** (consistent with the Gemma 3 tech report's KV-savings figure).
- gpt-oss-20b (24 L, 8 kv, head_dim 64, W=128, 12 full + 12 sliding): at 128K bf16 → **3.00 GiB** vs 6.0 GiB all-global.

### Hybrid SSM (Jamba, Nemotron-H, Granite-4.0, Falcon-H1, Qwen3-Next)

```
total = KV(attention layers only)  +  batch × SSM_state(constant, per mamba layer)
KV part: 2 × n_attn_layers × num_kv_heads × head_dim × dtype_bytes × ctx
Mamba1 state/layer  = [ d_inner×(d_conv−1)  +  d_inner×d_state ] × dtype_bytes
Mamba2 state/layer  = [ (d_inner + 2·n_groups·d_state)×(d_conv−1)  +  n_heads_ssm×head_dim_ssm×d_state ] × dtype_bytes
   where d_inner = mamba_expand × hidden_size
```
(vLLM `MambaStateShapeCalculator`: conv_state = `(intermediate_size + 2*n_groups*state_size, conv_kernel-1)`, temporal_state = `(num_heads, head_dim, state_size)`; Mamba1 conv = `(intermediate_size, conv_kernel-1)`, ssm = `(intermediate_size, state_size)`. MambaSpec page bytes = Σ prod(shape)×dtype_size. Some impls store d_conv instead of d_conv−1 — <0.1% difference.)

**Worked example (Jamba-v0.1 config: 32 layers, attn_layer_period=8 → 4 attention layers; 8 kv heads, head_dim=4096/32=128; mamba: d_state=16, d_conv=4, expand=2 → d_inner=8192):**
- KV @ 262,144 ctx fp16: 2×4×8×128×2×262144 = **4.295 GB ≈ the paper's published "4GB"** (vs Llama-2-70B 128GB, Mixtral 32GB in the same table — "8× smaller than a vanilla Transformer").
- SSM state: (8192×3 + 8192×16)×2 B ≈ 0.31 MB/layer × 28 mamba layers ≈ **8.7 MB per sequence, constant in ctx** — effectively negligible vs KV but scales with batch.

### Calculator assembly

```
VRAM ≈ weight_bytes + kv_total + activation/overhead
overhead heuristic: total ≈ 1.2 × weights (EleutherAI "≤20%"; HF accelerate "add up to an additional 20%")
plus framework allowances: CUDA context ~0.3-1 GB (estimate), vLLM preallocates KV to gpu_memory_utilization.
```

constants.name: bytes_per_param_fp16_bf16
constants.basis: EleutherAI transformer-math; llama.cpp F16 measured 16.0005 bpw

constants.name: bytes_per_param_fp8
constants.value: 1.0 B/param (8 bpw) + ~0.002 bpw if 128x128 block scales (DeepSeek style)
constants.basis: E4M3/E5M2 definition; DeepSeek-V3 fp32 scale per 128x128 block

constants.name: fp8_whole_checkpoint_effective_bpw_deepseek_v3
constants.basis: 688.586 GB HF checkpoint / 671e9 params (FP8 linears + BF16 norms/embeds + scales)

constants.name: gptq_awq_bpw_formula
constants.basis: fp16 scale (16b) + k-bit zero per group; AutoAWQ gemm.py pack layout (qweight in/8 int32, qzeros in/g/8 int32, scales in/g fp16)

constants.name: gptq_awq_int4_g128
constants.basis: 4+20/128; validated: predicted 5.727 GB vs actual 5.736 GB (GPTQ) / 5.728 GB (AWQ) for Llama-3.1-8B

constants.name: gptq_int4_gidx_actorder_overhead
constants.basis: int32 g_idx per input column, AutoGPTQ

constants.name: gptq_awq_unquantized_tensors
constants.value: embed_tokens + lm_head (+ norms) stay FP16: add 2 B x those params
constants.basis: AutoGPTQ/AutoAWQ never quantize embeddings/lm_head; confirmed by checkpoint-size reconstruction

constants.name: bnb_nf4_fp4_bs64
constants.basis: bitsandbytes blocksize 64, fp32 absmax per block

constants.name: gguf_theoretical_Q2_K
constants.value: 2.625 bpw (84 B / 256 w) — note: PR #1684 text says 2.5625, current struct is 2.625

constants.name: gguf_theoretical_MXFP4
constants.value: 4.25 bpw (17 B / 32 w; E2M1 + E8M0 scale per 32)
constants.basis: ggml-common.h static_assert; OCP MX v1.0 spec; gpt-oss model card '4.25 bits per parameter'

constants.name: gguf_theoretical_NVFP4
constants.value: 4.5 bpw (36 B / 64 w; E2M1 + UE4M3 scale per 16) + per-tensor fp32 scale in non-GGUF impls

constants.name: gguf_effective_IQ1_S
constants.basis: llama.cpp tools/quantize/README.md measured on Llama-3.1-8B (includes mix upgrades)

constants.name: gguf_effective_Q4_K_M
constants.value: 4.8944 bpw = 0.6118 B/param (the commonly quoted '~4.85 bits -> 0.606 B/param' is the same constant for older 7B Llamas)
constants.basis: llama.cpp quantize README; validated 8.0303e9 x 4.8944/8 = 4.58 GiB = published size

constants.name: gguf_mix_rule_output_weight
constants.value: Q6_K default (Q5_K for IQ1/IQ2/IQ3_XXS ftypes; Q8_0 if cols%256!=0 or MXFP4_MOE)

constants.name: gguf_mix_rule_token_embd
constants.value: base type, upgraded for low-bit ftypes (IQ2_XXS/XS/IQ1_*->Q2_K; IQ2_S/M,IQ3_XXS->IQ3_S; ternary->Q4_K); tied embeddings follow output rule

constants.name: kv_bytes_per_token_standard
constants.basis: vLLM v1 kv_cache_interface.py FullAttentionSpec (2*block_size*num_kv_heads*head_size*dtype_size); kipply 2*2*n_layers*n_heads*d_head; Raschka 4*num_kv_heads*head_dim per layer (bf16)

constants.name: kv_bytes_per_token_mla
constants.basis: vLLM v1 MLAAttentionSpec (coefficient 1); DeepSeek-V2/V3 architecture; Raschka formula

constants.name: kv_swa_tokens_per_layer
constants.value: full layers: min(ctx, max_ctx); sliding layers: min(ctx, sliding_window)
constants.basis: vLLM SlidingWindowSpec max_memory_usage_bytes uses min(sliding_window-1+batch_tokens, max_model_len)

constants.name: mamba1_state_per_layer
constants.confidence: exact (some impls use d_conv not d_conv-1; <0.1% diff)

constants.name: mamba2_state_per_layer
constants.basis: vLLM MambaStateShapeCalculator (mamba2): conv_dim = intermediate_size + 2*n_groups*state_size; temporal (num_heads, head_dim, state_size)

constants.name: kv_dtype_bytes
constants.value: fp16/bf16=2, fp8=1, gguf q8_0 KV=1.0625, gguf q4_0 KV=0.5625 B/element
constants.basis: llama.cpp cache-type quant blocks (8.5 and 4.5 bpw / 8)

constants.name: inference_overhead_multiplier
constants.basis: EleutherAI transformer-math 'Total Memory_Inference ~= 1.2x Model Memory, overhead <= 20%'; HF accelerate estimator docs cite same

constants.value: 10.41 GiB @ 131072 ctx (10 global + 52 local@1024, 16 kv, hd128, bf16) vs 62 GiB all-global = 6.0x saving

constants.value: 3.00 GiB @ 128K (12 full + 12 sliding@128; 8 kv, hd64, bf16)

constants.value: predicted 5.727 GB vs actual 5.736 GB (GPTQ) / 5.728 GB (AWQ)
constants.basis: 4.15625 bpw on 6.98B linear params + fp16 embed/head (1.05B); HF API blob sizes

constants.name: example_gptoss_20b_ckpt
constants.value: 13.76 GB (MXFP4 MoE @ 4.25 bpw + bf16 rest; 5.27 effective bpw whole-model)
constants.basis: HF API blob sizes for openai/gpt-oss-20b root safetensors

corrections.claim: fp8_whole_checkpoint_effective_bpw_deepseek_v3 — '8.21 effective bpw whole-model' (688.586 GB / 671e9 params)
corrections.was: 8.21 bpw labeled as whole-model effective bpw for DeepSeek-V3; implies weights-in-VRAM = 688.6 GB for the 671B model
corrections.should_be: The 688,586,727,753-byte checkpoint (163 safetensors, re-verified via HF API) contains 685B params, not 671B: official README states 'The total size of DeepSeek-V3 models on HuggingFace is 685B, which includes 671B of the Main Model weights and 14B of the Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) Module weights.' True effective bpw over stored params = 688.586e9*8/685e9 = 8.04 bpw. 8.21 is only valid as a file-size constant keyed to the headline 671B count. For a VRAM verdict, inference engines do not load the MTP module by default, so main-model weights in VRAM ≈ 671e9 × 8.04/8 ≈ 674.5 GB — about 14 GB less than the 688.6 GB checkpoint. Recommend storing 8.04 bpw × 685B (checkpoint/disk) and ≈674.5 GB (VRAM, main model only), or 8.21 bpw × 671B clearly labeled 'includes MTP module'.
corrections.source: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3/blob/main/README.md (line: 'total size ... is 685B ... 671B Main Model + 14B MTP'); https://huggingface.co/api/models/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3/tree/main?recursive=true (sum of .safetensors = 688,586,727,753 B)

corrections.claim: kv_bytes_per_token_standard basis cite — 'vLLM v1 FullAttentionSpec.page_size_bytes = 2*block_size*num_kv_heads*head_size*dtype_size'
corrections.was: Formula attributed to FullAttentionSpec.page_size_bytes
corrections.should_be: Numerically identical, but in current vLLM main the formula lives in AttentionSpec.real_page_size_bytes (2*block_size*num_kv_heads*head_size*dtype_size), and SlidingWindowSpec overrides it as block_size*num_kv_heads*(head_size+head_size_v)*dtype_size (equal to the factor-2 form when head_size_v==head_size, but supports asymmetric K/V head sizes). MLAAttentionSpec.real_page_size_bytes = storage_block_size*num_kv_heads*head_size*dtype_size with num_kv_heads=1, head_size=kv_lora_rank+qk_rope_head_dim=576 — confirms 'no factor 2' for MLA. Citation drift only; the calculator formulas themselves are correct.

missing: Measured effective bpw for IQ4_XS and IQ4_NL exist in the same llama.cpp tools/quantize/README.md table the researcher used but were omitted from their measured table: IQ4_XS = 4.4597 bpw (4.17 GiB on Llama-3.1-8B), IQ4_NL = 4.6818 bpw (4.38 GiB). Note both are well above their theoretical 4.25/4.5 bpw due to mix upgrades — important for the calculator since IQ4_XS is a popular ftype.
ggml master now also defines block_q1_0 (QK1_0=128, block = 2+128/8 = 18 B → 1.125 bpw exact, ggml-common.h static_assert line 182) — a 1-bit type absent from the researcher's GGUF ladder.

vLLM fp8 MLA KV-cache layouts: with kv-cache-dtype 'fp8_ds_mla' the per-token-per-layer cost is a fixed 656 B for DeepSeek V3.2-style MLA (custom layout for kv_lora_rank=512 + qk_rope_head_dim=64) and 584 B for DeepSeek-V4 (448B NoPE + 128B RoPE + 8B fp8 scale), per MLAAttentionSpec.real_page_size_bytes in kv_cache_interface.py. Naively scaling the researcher's MLA formula by dtype_bytes=1 gives 576 B/token — wrong for these engines. Also SlidingWindowMLASpec exists (sliding-window MLA).

Chunked local attention (Llama 4): vLLM ChunkedLocalAttentionSpec retains min(attention_chunk_size + batch_tokens, max_model_len) tokens per chunked layer — a third interleaved-attention pattern besides full and sliding-window, not covered by the researcher's SWA formula.

Quantized-KV scale overhead in vLLM: when kv_quant_mode is per-token-head, page_size_bytes adds 2 * block_size * num_kv_heads * 4 bytes (fp32 K and V scales per token per head) carved from the KV allocation (AttentionSpec.page_size_bytes). For fp8 KV with head_dim 128 this is a ~3% adder the calculator ignores.

Linear-attention state for Qwen3-Next/GatedDeltaNet hybrids: researcher lists Qwen3-Next under 'Hybrid SSM' but only gives Mamba1/Mamba2 formulas; vLLM MambaStateShapeCalculator.linear_attention_state_shape is (num_heads, head_dim, head_dim) per linear layer (plus conv state), a different constant-state formula.

Measured effective bpw for legacy GGUF ftypes Q4_0/Q4_1/Q5_0/Q5_1 are not in the README table; the calculator must fall back to theoretical bpw + mix rules for these (worth an explicit note so it doesn't silently use Q4_K_M-style measured values).
verification.summary: Adversarially re-verified against primary sources fetched fresh on 2026-06-12: ggml-common.h static_asserts (all 23 theoretical GGUF bpw values recomputed and confirmed exact, incl. Q2_K=84B=2.625 bpw, MXFP4 17B/32w=4.25, NVFP4 QK=64/SUB=16/UE4M3 scales=36B/64w=4.5, TQ1_0=54B, IQ1_M=56B); llama.cpp tools/quantize/README.md (all 22 quoted measured bpw + GiB sizes match exactly, table is indeed Llama-3.1-8B); llama-quant.cpp mix rules (output→Q6_K default / Q5_K for IQ1-IQ2-IQ3_XXS family / Q8_0 for MXFP4_MOE or nx%block!=0; untied token_embd upgrades IQ2_XXS/XS/IQ1→Q2_K, IQ2_S/M+IQ3_XXS→IQ3_S, ternary→Q4_K; attn_v Q2_K→Q4_K at GQA>=4; first 1/8 ffn_down upgraded — all confirmed verbatim); vLLM v1 kv_cache_interface.py (factor-2 standard KV, MLA coefficient 1 with head 576, SWA min(W-1+batch, max_model_len)) and mamba_utils.py (Mamba1/Mamba2 conv/temporal shapes incl. conv_kernel-1 and conv_dim=intermediate+2*n_groups*state) — all match. HF configs re-fetched and confirmed: DeepSeek-V3 (61L/512/64, fp8 128x128 blocks), gpt-oss-20b (24L, 8kv, hd64, W=128, 12 sliding+12 full, 131072 max ctx), Gemma-3-27B (62L, 16kv, hd128, W=1024, pattern 6 → 10 global+52 local), Jamba-v0.1 (32L, period 8/offset 4 → 4 attn layers; d_state 16, d_conv 4, expand 2, 262144 ctx), Qwen3-8B (36L, 8kv, hd128), Llama-3.1-70B (80L, 8kv, hidden 8192/64 heads → hd128), Llama-3.1-8B (32L, 8kv, untied, vocab 128256). Checkpoint sizes re-summed via HF API: DeepSeek-V3 = 688,586,727,753 B, gpt-oss-20b = 13.761 GB, hugging-quants GPTQ-INT4 = 5.736 GB, AWQ-INT4 = 5.728 GB — all exactly as claimed; GPTQ/AWQ k+(16+k)/g formula and 4.15625 bpw validation hold. Raschka's published 144 KiB (Qwen3-8B) and 68.6 KiB (DeepSeek-V3) confirmed, and all worked-example arithmetic recomputed correctly. The one substantive error: DeepSeek-V3 '8.21 effective bpw whole-model' divides a 685B-param checkpoint (671B main + 14B MTP, per the official README) by 671B — true stored-param bpw is 8.04, and main-model VRAM is ~674.5 GB, not 688.6 GB. Everything else is clean; the missing list covers IQ4_XS/IQ4_NL measured bpw, ggml q1_0, vLLM fp8 MLA layouts (656/584 B per token), Llama-4 chunked local attention, per-token fp8-KV scale overhead, and Qwen3-Next linear-attention state. Spot-check coverage exceeded half of all numeric claims, including every surprising one. Local copies of fetched sources: /tmp/ggml-common.h, /tmp/quantize-README.md, /tmp/llama-quant.cpp, /tmp/kv_cache_interface.py, /tmp/mamba_utils.py, /tmp/dsv3.json, /tmp/gptoss20b.json, /tmp/jamba.json, /tmp/qwen3-8b.json, /tmp/gemma3-27b.json.

research.findings:
# VRAM Calculator Math — Topic 2: Everything Around the Core Terms

All numbers verified against primary sources June 12, 2026. Estimates are explicitly marked **(estimate)**.

## (a) Inference-time activation memory

### What it scales with

Activation (scratch) memory at inference scales **linearly with the number of tokens processed in one forward pass** ("batched tokens" = sum across all sequences of tokens scheduled this step), times hidden size, times dtype bytes, times an architecture coefficient dominated by the MLP intermediates. It does **not** scale with total context length when FlashAttention-style kernels are used (the S×S score matrix is never materialized), and it does **not** accumulate per layer at inference (layer buffers are reused; only the largest layer's live set matters — gguf-parser explicitly models "only the usage of the largest layer").

### The cap: chunked prefill / token budget

Modern servers cap per-step batched tokens, which caps activation peak:

- **vLLM V1** (chunked prefill on by default): `max_num_batched_tokens` defaults are usage/hardware dependent (`vllm/engine/arg_utils.py`):
  - H100/MI300-class (≥70 GiB, non-A100): **16384** offline `LLM` class / **8192** API server; `max_num_seqs` = **1024**
  - other GPUs (incl. A100): **8192** offline / **2048** server; `max_num_seqs` = **256**
  - hard floors in `vllm/config/scheduler.py`: `DEFAULT_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS = 2048`, `DEFAULT_MAX_NUM_SEQS = 128`
- **SGLang** `chunked_prefill_size` by GPU tier (`server_args.py`): <20 GiB → 2048; <35 GiB → 2048; <60 GiB → 4096; <160 GiB → 8192; B200/MI300 → 16384.
- **llama.cpp**: logical batch `n_batch` = 2048, **physical micro-batch `n_ubatch` = 512** (`common/common.h`); compute-graph buffers scale with `n_ubatch`, not full context (except attention-mask/score terms, see below).

### How vLLM actually gets the number (empirical, not analytic)

vLLM does **not** use a formula — it runs a dummy forward pass at `max_num_batched_tokens` during startup and measures `torch.cuda.memory_stats()["allocated_bytes.all.peak"]`. KV cache then gets whatever is left:

```
kv_cache_bytes = total_gpu_mem * gpu_memory_utilization
               - weights - activation_peak(measured) - non_torch(measured)
```

Measured log examples (vLLM PR #10511 and forum):
- `model weights take 14.96GiB; non_torch_memory takes 0.18GiB; PyTorch activation peak memory takes 1.26GiB; ... KV Cache is 54.90GiB` (8B-class bf16, 80 GiB GPU)
- `model weights take 27.59GiB; non_torch_memory takes 0.09GiB; PyTorch activation peak memory takes 1.48GiB`

### Analytic formula for a calculator (estimate, calibrated to the above)

```
act_peak_bytes ≈ n_batched_tokens × dtype_bytes × (2·hidden_size + 3·d_ff_active)   # FFN live set: residual + normed + up + gate + act
              + logits_rows × vocab_size × 4                                        # fp32 logits; servers compute only sampled rows (≤ max_num_seqs)
all × allocator_slack ≈ 1.3–1.5                                                     # PyTorch caching-allocator + attention workspace slack (estimate)
```

Sanity check vs vLLM measurement: Llama-3-8B (h=4096, d_ff=14336, bf16) at 8192 tokens → 8192 × 2 × (8192+43008) ≈ 0.78 GiB; ×1.4 slack + logits ≈ 1.2–1.3 GiB, matching the measured 1.26 GiB.

For **non-FlashAttention** paths (llama.cpp default off on some backends), add the score matrix, which gguf-parser models exactly as the dominant graph tensor:

```
kq = 4 bytes × n_kv × n_ubatch × n_attention_heads        # F32 [n_kv, n_tokens, n_heads] — grows with context!
FA replaces it with: 2 bytes × n_kv × n_ubatch            # F16 mask
```

llama.cpp also keeps a **logits/output buffer** (gguf-parser, from llama.cpp source):

```
output_buffer = n_outputs × embedding_length × 4  +  n_outputs × vocab_size × 4   # fp32
```

### Realistic magnitudes

| Setting | Activation scratch |
|---|---|
| Single request, 7–8B, llama.cpp, n_ubatch=512, FA on | ~0.3–0.6 GiB **(estimate)** |
| vLLM server, 8B bf16, 8192-token budget | 1.26 GiB (measured) |
| vLLM server, ~30B-class weights | 1.48 GiB (measured) |
| SGLang conservative reserve (act + graphs + slack), H100 | `512 MiB + 1.5 MiB/token × 8192 + 2 MiB × 256 ≈ 13 GiB`, floored at **10 GiB** on >60 GiB GPUs |

SGLang's own docstring: *"The activation memory is proportional to the chunked_prefill_size. The cuda graph memory is proportional to the decode max_bs. We use reserved_mem = chunked_prefill_size * 1.5 + max_bs * 2 ... The coefficient 1.5 is a heuristic value."* (units: MiB). Note SGLang's 1.5 MiB/token is ~10× vLLM's measured ~0.16 MiB/token for an 8B model because it must also cover huge-vocab logits, MLA backends, DP-attention padding, and allocator inefficiency on DeepSeek-class models — treat it as the conservative upper band.

### EleutherAI training-side activation formulas (for completeness / calculator's training mode)

```
no recompute:        act = s·b·h·L · (10 + 24/t + 5·a·s/(h·t))  bytes
selective recompute: act = s·b·h·L · (10 + 24/t)                bytes
full recompute:      act = 2 · s·b·h·L                          bytes
(s=seq len, b=batch, h=hidden, L=layers, a=heads, t=TP degree)
```

## (b) Fixed runtime overheads

| Component | Size | Source |
|---|---|---|
| CUDA context, RTX 4090 | **384 MiB** | vLLM issue #12059 |
| CUDA context, H100 | **~520 MiB** | vLLM issue #12059 |
| CUDA context, calculator default | **0.5 GiB** (vLLM's own proposed mitigation subtracts `0.5 GiB`) | vLLM issue #12059 |
| PyTorch CUDA kernels (legacy, pre-lazy-loading) | "1–2 GB"; measured **1343 MB** on V100 just for `torch.ones((1,1)).to("cuda")` | HF transformers model_memory_anatomy (v4.49 docs). Modern CUDA ≥11.8 lazy loading brings this down to the ~0.4–0.6 GiB context numbers above **(estimate)** |
| NCCL buffers (TP>1), per GPU | **0.5 GiB** after init → **~1 GiB** incl. attention-backend buffers at peak | vLLM `memory_profiling` docstring quantitative example (v0.7.3 `vllm/utils.py`) |
| non-torch memory, single GPU (no TP) | **0.09–0.18 GiB** measured | vLLM PR #10511 logs |
| CUDA graphs (vLLM decode graphs, default capture sizes) | **0.21–0.64 GiB** typical; up to **6.56 GiB** in large/full-graph configs | vLLM "Graph capturing finished in N secs, took X GiB" logs |
| CUDA graphs (SGLang heuristic) | **2 MiB × cuda_graph_max_bs** (max_bs default 256 on H100 → 512 MiB; 512 for TP≥4 or B200); piecewise prefill graphs **+8 MiB per capture size**; MLA backend **+1.5 GiB** | sglang `server_args.py` |
| Framework constant metadata (SGLang) | **512 MiB** | sglang `server_args.py` |
| Speculative decoding reserve (SGLang) | EAGLE **+4 GiB**, standalone draft **+6 GiB** | sglang `server_args.py` |
| Multimodal processor cache (vLLM) | **4 GiB** default (`mm_processor_cache_gb`) | vLLM docs |
| llama.cpp bootstrap footprint | 5 MiB model-load + GGUF metadata + tokenizer maps (`tokens × (4+4+32+24+32) bytes`, +GPT-2 merges `×(48+56)`); scheduler 4 MiB; graph metadata = `tensor_overhead × max(1024, 8 × n_tensors)` | gguf-parser-go `file_estimate__llamacpp.go` |
| gguf-parser "platform footprint" | **150 MiB RAM + 250 MiB VRAM** (NonUMA default `--platform-footprint "150,250"`) | gguf-parser-go `cmd/gguf-parser/main.go` |

Suggested calculator decomposition for "fixed overhead per GPU":
```
fixed_overhead = cuda_context (0.5 GiB)
              + framework_buffers (0.1–0.5 GiB single GPU; +0.5–1 GiB NCCL if TP/PP > 1)
              + cuda_graphs (0 if eager; 0.3–0.7 GiB typical; 2 MiB × max_decode_bs as scaling rule)
```

## (c) Fragmentation / safety margins in real deployments

- **vLLM** `gpu_memory_utilization` default = **0.92** in current main (`vllm/config/cache.py`, `Field(default=0.92)`); long-standing prior default was **0.90**. I.e., vLLM deliberately leaves 8–10% of the card unmanaged — that slack absorbs CUDA-graph capture, allocator fragmentation, and other processes' contexts.
- **SGLang** `mem_fraction_static` = `(gpu_mem − reserved_mem)/gpu_mem`, with `reserved_mem` per the formula in (a)/(b); fallback **0.88** when GPU memory is unknown; **minimum 10 GiB reserved** on >60 GiB GPUs (≈12.5% of an 80 GiB H100). Extra `tp_size × pp_size / 8` GiB reserved for parallelism.
- **gguf-parser/llama.cpp** ecosystem: estimates "usually deviate from actual usage by about **100 MiB**"; recommends platform footprint reserve 150 MiB RAM / 250 MiB VRAM.
- **PyTorch fragmentation**: vLLM PR #10511 notes repeated model loads show varying non-torch memory "due to CUDA's internal allocation patterns"; standard mitigation `PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True`.
- Calculator recommendation **(estimate)**: flag a config as "won't fit" unless `required ≤ 0.92 × VRAM` (server) or `≤ 0.95 × VRAM − 0.4 GiB` (single-GPU desktop where the display/compositor also holds memory).

## (d) Survey of public VRAM calculators

| Calculator | Decomposition | Coefficients |
|---|---|---|
| **EleutherAI transformer-math** | Inference: `Total ≈ 1.2 × weights`. Training: weights + optimizer + gradients + activations | weights 4/2/1 B/param (fp32/fp16/int8); AdamW **12 B/param**, 8-bit Adam **6**, SGD-m **8**; grads 4 (or 2); activation `sbhL(10 + 24/t + 5as/ht)` |
| **kipply (transformer-inference-arithmetic)** | weights + KV cache; activations declared **"negligible"** for capacity | weights 2 B/param fp16; **KV/token = 2·2·n_layers·n_heads·d_head = 4·n_layers·d_model bytes** (fp16, MHA); 52B example: ~0.002 GB/token |
| **HF accelerate `estimate-memory`** | dtype-converted state-dict on `meta` device; reports **largest layer**, **total size**, **"Training using Adam" = 4 × total** | loading accuracy "within a few %" (bert: est 413.18 vs actual 413.68 MB); inference: *"expect to add up to an additional 20% as found by EleutherAI"*; no KV cache, no activations, no CUDA context |
| **apxml VRAM calculator** (methodology per author's twm.me guide) | `Base = weights + activations + KV + optimizer`, then `Total = Base × modality_overhead` | weights 4/2/2/1/0.5 B/param (fp32/fp16/bf16/int8/int4); **KV/token = 2 × hidden × layers × bytes_el**; **activations = 0.2 × weights (inference)**, ~2× (training); optimizer = 4× weights; modality multipliers: text **1.15×**, reasoning **1.25×**, audio **1.3×**, video **1.4×**, multimodal **1.5×+**; site claims results approximate (±15% per third-party reviews) |
| **gguf-parser-go (llama.cpp ecosystem, used by GPUStack)** | Exact graph reconstruction per device: weights (per offloaded layer, real tensor bytes) + KV (`(k_row + v_row) × n_kv` per layer, cache-dtype aware) + compute graph (largest layer's actual tensors: `kq = F32[n_kv, n_ubatch, n_heads]` non-FA / F16 mask FA; FFN `F32[d_ff, n_ubatch]` per gate/up/norm; MoE router terms) + output buffer `n_outputs×(vocab+embd)×4` + bootstrap footprint | platform footprint 150/250 MiB; graph metadata `overhead × max(1024, 8·n_tensors)` + 4 MiB scheduler; accuracy ±~100 MiB |
| **vLLM (implicit calculator)** | `KV = total×util − weights − act_peak(measured) − non_torch(measured)`; CUDA graphs live in the (1−util) slack | util default 0.92; measured act ≈ 1.3–1.5 GiB at 8k token budget |
| **SGLang (implicit calculator)** | `mem_fraction_static = 1 − reserved/total`; `reserved = 512 MiB + 1.5 MiB×chunked_prefill_size + 2 MiB×max_bs + tp·pp/8 GiB (+DP, +MLA, +spec-decode terms), min 10 GiB on big GPUs` | see constants table |

Key divergence to note in docs: HF accelerate measures **only loading**; EleutherAI/kipply add a flat ~1.2× or treat activations as negligible; apxml uses weight-proportional activations (0.2×) which is wrong in shape (activations scale with batched tokens × hidden, not with parameter count) but lands in the right range for 1 request at moderate context; gguf-parser/vLLM/SGLang are the only ones modeling the true token-budget scaling.

## (e) Multi-GPU tensor parallelism

**Sharded ÷ TP:** attention QKV/O projections (column/row parallel), MLP up/gate/down, **KV cache (by KV head)**, and in vLLM also the embedding + LM head (vocab-parallel).

**Replicated (per rank):** LayerNorm/RMSNorm params (negligible bytes); the **residual-stream activations** — every rank holds the full `[tokens, hidden]` tensor after each all-reduce. EleutherAI's activation formula encodes exactly this: the `10·sbhL` term is *not* divided by `t` while the `24/t` term is. So per-GPU activation memory shrinks sublinearly with TP:

```
act_per_gpu ≈ act_total × (r + (1−r)/TP),  r ≈ 0.3 (replicated fraction, estimate from 10/(10+24))
```

**KV-cache replication trap (GQA):** vLLM `get_num_kv_heads = max(1, total_kv_heads // tp_size)` — when `tp_size > num_kv_heads`, KV heads are **replicated** so each GPU keeps ≥1 head. An 8-KV-head model (Llama-3 70B) at TP=16 stores 2× the total KV bytes of TP=8; per-GPU KV stops shrinking at TP = num_kv_heads. MLA models (DeepSeek) decode as MQA (`num_kv_heads = 1`), so KV is fully replicated across TP ranks (which is why DP-attention exists).

**Per-device overhead under TP** (each rank pays all of (b) again):

```
per_gpu_overhead(TP>1) = cuda_context (0.5 GiB) + NCCL (0.5–1 GiB) + cuda_graphs (0.3–0.7 GiB) ≈ 1.3–2.2 GiB  (estimate)
per_gpu_overhead(TP=1) ≈ 0.6–1.2 GiB  (estimate)
```

SGLang's production rule of thumb is the additive `tp_size × pp_size / 8` GiB on top of its base reserve. A practical calculator formula:

```
fits if:  weights/TP + kv_total/min(TP, num_kv_heads)/ (TP/min(TP,num_kv_heads))  [i.e. kv_per_gpu]
        + act_per_gpu + per_gpu_overhead  ≤  margin × VRAM_per_gpu
kv_per_gpu = kv_per_token × max(1, num_kv_heads/TP)/num_kv_heads × ... — simplest correct form:
kv_per_gpu = seq_tokens × 2 × layers × max(1, num_kv_heads/TP) × head_dim × kv_dtype_bytes
```

constants.name: bytes_per_param.gguf_q4_k_m
constants.value: 4.85 bits ≈ 0.606 B/param (weights-only; add ~3-5% GGUF metadata/non-quantized tensors)
constants.basis: llama.cpp k-quants (PR #1684) bpw table; topic-1 territory, included for completeness

constants.name: kv_bytes_per_token
constants.value: 2 (K,V) x n_layers x n_kv_heads x head_dim x kv_dtype_bytes; MHA fp16 shortcut = 4 x n_layers x d_model

constants.name: inference_total_overhead_factor
constants.basis: EleutherAI transformer-math 'Total Memory_Inference ≈ 1.2 x Model Memory'; HF accelerate docs cite the same +20%
constants.confidence: high (as a heuristic; crude vs token-budget model)

constants.name: act_bytes_per_batched_token.analytic
constants.value: dtype_bytes x (2*hidden + 3*d_ff_active) per token, x1.3-1.5 allocator slack, + logits_rows x vocab x 4 B
constants.basis: Derived from per-layer live set; calibrated to vLLM measured 1.26 GiB @ 8192 tokens for Llama-3-8B (PR #10511 log)
constants.confidence: medium (estimate, calibrated to one architecture family)

constants.name: act_peak.vllm_measured_8B_8ktokens
constants.basis: vLLM PR #10511 log lines and vLLM forum-reported logs

constants.name: act_reserve.sglang_per_prefill_token
constants.value: 1.5 MiB per chunked-prefill token (conservative, includes logits/MLA/DP slack)
constants.confidence: high (it is the shipped heuristic; conservative upper band)

constants.name: max_num_batched_tokens.vllm_defaults
constants.value: H100-class: 16384 offline / 8192 server; A100 & smaller: 8192 / 2048; floor 2048

constants.name: llamacpp.attn_scores_nonFA
constants.value: 4 B x n_kv x n_ubatch x n_heads (F32 kq tensor; dominant graph term, grows with context); FA replaces with 2 B x n_kv x n_ubatch

constants.name: llamacpp.output_buffer
constants.basis: gguf-parser-go output buffer formula, from llama.cpp source

constants.name: cuda_context.default_reserve
constants.confidence: high (as a default; varies 0.3-1 GiB by GPU/driver)

constants.name: torch_cuda_kernels.legacy_upper_bound
constants.value: 1-2 GB (measured 1343 MB on V100); modern lazy loading => use cuda_context number instead

constants.name: nccl_buffers_per_gpu
constants.value: 0.5 GiB after init, ~1 GiB peak incl. attention-backend buffers (TP>1 only)
constants.basis: vLLM memory_profiling docstring quantitative example (vllm/utils.py v0.7.3)

constants.name: cudagraph_memory.vllm_typical
constants.value: 0.21-0.64 GiB typical; up to 6.56 GiB worst observed
constants.basis: vLLM 'Graph capturing finished... took X GiB' logs (PR #11349 era logs, huikang.dev log guide)

constants.name: cudagraph_memory.sglang_rule
constants.value: 2 MiB per captured decode batch slot (max_bs 8/24/32/256/512 by GPU tier); +8 MiB per piecewise-prefill capture size; +1.5 GiB MLA backend

constants.name: framework_constant_metadata.sglang
constants.basis: sglang server_args.py 'Constant meta data (e.g., from attention backend)'

constants.name: tp_parallel_reserve.sglang
constants.value: tp_size x pp_size / 8 GiB extra per GPU

constants.name: big_gpu_reserve_floor.sglang
constants.value: 10 GiB minimum reserved on GPUs > 60 GiB

constants.name: gpu_memory_utilization.vllm_default
constants.value: 0.92 (current main; was 0.90 for most of vLLM's history)

constants.name: mem_fraction_static.sglang_fallback
constants.value: 0.88 when GPU memory unknown; otherwise computed from reserved_mem

constants.name: safety_margin.recommended
constants.value: 8-10% of total VRAM for servers (mirror vLLM 0.92/0.90); 5% + 0.4 GiB for desktop single-GPU (display compositor)
constants.basis: vLLM/SGLang defaults; estimate for desktop case

constants.name: estimate_error_band.ggufparser
constants.value: ±100 MiB typical deviation; platform footprint default 150 MiB RAM + 250 MiB VRAM

constants.name: llamacpp.bootstrap_footprint
constants.value: 5 MiB model load + GGUF metadata + tokenizer maps (~tokens x 96 B) + 4 MiB scheduler + tensor_overhead x max(1024, 8 x n_tensors) graph metadata
constants.basis: gguf-parser-go file_estimate__llamacpp.go with llama.cpp source links

constants.name: tp.kv_heads_per_gpu
constants.value: max(1, total_kv_heads // tp_size) — KV replicated when tp > kv_heads; MLA decodes as 1 KV head (fully replicated)

constants.name: tp.activation_replicated_fraction
constants.basis: EleutherAI activation formula structure sbhL(10 + 24/t): the 10-term is TP-replicated, 24-term sharded; r=10/34≈0.3

constants.name: tp.per_gpu_fixed_overhead
constants.value: 1.3-2.2 GiB per GPU when TP>1 (context 0.5 + NCCL 0.5-1 + cudagraphs 0.3-0.7); 0.6-1.2 GiB at TP=1
constants.basis: Sum of measured components above (vLLM issue #12059, memory_profiling docstring, capture logs)

constants.name: optimizer_bytes_per_param
constants.value: AdamW 12 B/param; 8-bit Adam 6; SGD-momentum 8; HF accelerate shortcut: training_total = 4 x model_size

constants.name: apxml.activation_factor
constants.value: 0.2 x weights (inference), ~2 x weights (training) — weight-proportional heuristic, wrong scaling shape but included for cross-checking
constants.basis: twm.me advanced VRAM guide (apxml calculator author)
constants.confidence: high (that it's their formula); low (as physics)

constants.name: training_activation_formula
constants.value: sbhL(10 + 24/t + 5as/(ht)) B no-recompute; sbhL(10+24/t) selective; 2sbhL full recompute

sources: https://blog.huikang.dev/2025/12/28/vllm-logs.html (Graph capturing finished ... took 0.21/0.64/6.56 GiB log examples)

corrections.claim: act_peak.vllm_measured_8B_8ktokens — second data point labeled '1.48 GiB (~30B-class)' / table row 'vLLM server, ~30B-class weights'
corrections.was: 1.48 GiB activation peak attributed to a '~30B-class' model
corrections.should_be: The log exists verbatim (vLLM discussion #3056): 'model weights take 27.59GiB; non_torch_memory takes 0.09GiB; PyTorch activation peak memory takes 1.48GiB; the rest of the memory reserved for KV Cache is 6.38GiB' (35.55 GiB budget). The source does not identify the model; 27.59 GiB of weights corresponds to ~14-15B params at bf16 (e.g., Qwen-14B class), not ~30B, unless it was ~8-bit quantized (unstated). Relabel as 'model with 27.59 GiB weights' and drop the 30B-class inference.
corrections.confidence: high that the label is unsupported; medium that the model is ~14B-class

corrections.claim: spec_decode_reserve.sglang — '+4 GiB (EAGLE)'
corrections.was: EAGLE +4 GiB, standalone draft +6 GiB
corrections.should_be: STANDALONE +6 GiB; ALL other speculative algorithms except NGRAM and DFLASH get +4 GiB (EAGLE is the main case but the condition is `speculative_algorithm not in {"NGRAM", "DFLASH"}`, so MTP-style algorithms also get +4 GiB; NGRAM/DFLASH get +0). Magnitudes are correct.

corrections.claim: cudagraph_memory.vllm_typical — '0.21-0.64 GiB typical'
corrections.was: 0.21-0.64 GiB typical; up to 6.56 GiB
corrections.should_be: Range corroborated but low end differs: primary logs show 'took 0.14 GiB' and 'took 6.56 GiB' (vLLM PR #11349 / issue #11452 era) and 'Graph capturing finished in 9 secs, took 0.64 GiB' (huikang.dev). The exact 0.21 figure was not found in any primary source; use 0.14-0.64 GiB typical, up to 6.56 GiB.
corrections.confidence: medium (0.21 may exist in an unindexed log; 0.14 and 6.56 verified)

corrections.claim: Section (a) table row 'SGLang conservative reserve ... ≈ 13 GiB, floored at 10 GiB on >60 GiB GPUs' (wording)
corrections.was: '≈ 13 GiB, floored at 10 GiB' reads as if the reserve is reduced to 10 GiB
corrections.should_be: The 10 GiB is a MINIMUM (`reserved_mem = max(reserved_mem, 10*1024)`), so a computed 13 GiB reserve stays 13 GiB (plus tp_size*pp_size/8 GiB, +128 MiB at TP=1, omitted from the example sum). Section (c) states it correctly; the table wording should say 'minimum 10 GiB'.

corrections.claim: multimodal_processor_cache.vllm — '4 GiB default'
corrections.was: 4 GiB default, presented as a single per-GPU overhead
corrections.should_be: Default is 4 (GiB) per process, but the current docstring states the cache 'is duplicated for each API process and engine core process, resulting in a total memory usage of mm_processor_cache_gb * (api_server_count + data_parallel_size)' — and it is host/process memory tied to the processor, not a guaranteed single 4 GiB VRAM line item. A calculator should multiply by process count.

corrections.claim: Section (a) 'non-FlashAttention paths (llama.cpp default off on some backends)'
corrections.was: implies FA is default-off in llama.cpp
corrections.should_be: Current llama.cpp master defaults to LLAMA_FLASH_ATTN_TYPE_AUTO (common/common.h line 473) — FA is enabled by default wherever the backend/model supports it and falls back otherwise. The F32 kq term applies only to unsupported backend/quant combinations; a calculator should treat FA-on as the llama.cpp default in 2026.

missing: Unified-memory caps entirely absent despite being core to can-I-run-it verdicts: Apple Silicon Metal limits GPU-visible memory to recommendedMaxWorkingSetSize (~65-75% of system RAM by default, overridable via sysctl iogpu.wired_limit_mb); llama.cpp/Ollama Metal builds budget against this, not total RAM. A Mac entry is required alongside the discrete-GPU margins.

Ollama (the most widely used llama.cpp deployment) ships different defaults than upstream llama.cpp: num_batch=512 (not 2048), default context 4096 (recent versions; was 2048), flash attention and KV-cache quantization gated by env vars — its activation/KV numbers differ from the raw llama.cpp row in the survey.

vLLM PR #10511's own TP=2 sanity-check log shows non_torch_memory = 1.84 GiB per rank (vs 0.18 GiB at TP=1) — direct primary evidence that the 0.5-1 GiB NCCL band from the memory_profiling docstring understates real TP overhead; the 1.3-2.2 GiB per-GPU TP>1 estimate should be widened or anchored to this measurement (https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/10511 comments).

vLLM V1's default cudagraph_mode is now FULL_AND_PIECEWISE (full graphs for decode + piecewise for prefill), which is the regime behind the multi-GiB capture numbers; the survey only mentions 'large/full-graph configs' without naming the default.

SGLang fallback when GPU memory cannot be detected: chunked_prefill_size=4096 and decode max_bs=160 (in addition to the 0.88 mem_fraction fallback the researcher did capture) — server_args.py lines ~1673-1678.

SGLang decode cuda-graph max_bs is TP-dependent in mid tiers: 24→80 (<35 GiB) and 32→160 (<60 GiB) when tp_size≥4, and there are two distinct 8192-token tiers (<90 GiB and <160 GiB); the researcher's '8/24/32/256/512' list collapses these.
vLLM TPU-specific batch-token defaults (V6E: 2048/1024, V5E: 1024/512 by usage context) exist in the same get_batch_defaults function — relevant if the calculator covers TPU.

PagedAttention KV block granularity: up to one partially-filled block (block_size 16 tokens default) wasted per sequence — the residual fragmentation term inside the KV pool that none of the surveyed formulas model (negligible except at huge max_num_seqs).
verification.summary: Re-verified ~30 of the ~45 numeric claims directly against primary sources fetched 2026-06-12, prioritizing every surprising number. Confirmed exactly: vLLM gpu_memory_utilization default 0.92 (vllm/config/cache.py main), scheduler floors 2048/128, get_batch_defaults 16384/8192 + max_num_seqs 1024 for >=70GiB non-A100 GPUs and 8192/2048 + 256 otherwise (arg_utils.py), get_num_kv_heads = max(1, total//tp) with MLA->1 (config/model.py); all SGLang reserved_mem constants (512 MiB base, 1.5 MiB/token with 2048 floor, 2 MiB x max_bs, tp*pp/8 GiB, len(prefill bs)x8 MiB piecewise, +1.5 GiB MLA, 10 GiB minimum on >60 GiB GPUs, 0.88 fallback) and the chunked_prefill_size/max_bs tiers (server_args.py); llama.cpp n_batch=2048/n_ubatch=512 (common/common.h); vLLM issue #12059 (384 MiB 4090, ~520 MiB H100, 0.5 GiB mitigation); PR #10511 logs (14.96/0.18/1.26/54.90 GiB verbatim); v0.7.3 memory_profiling docstring (NCCL 0.5->1 GiB); EleutherAI formulas (1.2x, 12/6/8 B/param optimizers, sbhL(10+24/t+5as/ht)); kipply (2*2*layers*heads*d_head, 52B 4*64*8192=0.002 GB/token, 'negligible' intermediates); HF accelerate (413.18 vs 413.68 MB, Adam=4x, +20% EleutherAI quote); HF transformers v4.49 (1-2GB kernels, 1343 MB V100); gguf-parser-go (~100 MiB deviation, '150,250' footprint default, kq=F32[n_kv,n_tokens,n_heads], FA mask F16[n_kv,n_tokens], output buffer (vocab+embd)*n_outputs*4, 5 MiB+metadata+token maps footprint, 4 MiB scheduler, maxNodes=max(1024,8*n_tensors)); vLLM mm_processor_cache_gb=4; twm.me/apxml multipliers (1.15/1.25/1.3/1.4/1.5+, 0.2x/2x/4x). Six corrections, all minor: the '~30B-class' label on the 1.48 GiB measurement is unsupported (27.59 GiB weights ~= 14-15B bf16); SGLang +4 GiB applies to all non-NGRAM/DFLASH spec algorithms, not just EAGLE; CUDA-graph low end is 0.14 GiB in verified logs (0.21 unfound); the '13 GiB floored at 10 GiB' wording inverts the floor semantics; mm processor cache multiplies per process; llama.cpp FA is now default-AUTO (on where supported). Main gaps: no unified-memory (Apple Silicon) coverage, no Ollama-default divergence, and the TP=2 measured non_torch of 1.84 GiB that undercuts the researcher's own NCCL band. Core constants are reliable for direct use.
